what: How do you fit the adapted model?
title: Feature-RSA Across States: Domain Adaptation from Encoding to Recall
text: direct workflow uses fixed target representation lets model adapt mapping target-train rows . Use mode = "stacked" want one shared mapping estimated source rows plus target-train rows. Use mode = "coupled" expect real state shift want separate source target weights tied together rho.
code: coupled_spec <- feature_rsa_da_model(   dataset = da_example$dataset,   design = da_example$design_fixed,   mode = "coupled",   lambdas = c(low = 0.1, semantic = 0.1),   alpha_target = 0.3,   rho = 3,   rsa_simfun = "spearman" )  coupled_res <- run_regional(coupled_spec, da_example$region_mask)  coupled_res$performance_table[, c(   "target_pattern_correlation",   "target_rdm_correlation",   "target_r2_full" )] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   target_pattern_correlation target_rdm_correlation target_r2_full #>                        <dbl>                  <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1                      0.963                  0.939          0.813

what: How do you keep the matching step unbiased?
title: Feature-RSA Across States: Domain Adaptation from Encoding to Recall
text: important new piece target_builder. Use target-side representation fixed ahead time, estimated recall data. callback fold-aware. receives train_idx test_idx outer target fold, must return target predictors original target row order. vignette builder simple train-fold-recentering step. real watch--recall pipeline, plug row-matching alignment routine. right pattern whenever target-side alignment otherwise leak held-recall rows analysis. model still evaluates held-target rows, now target feature construction also fold-aware.
code: target_builder <- function(X_train, train_idx, builder_data) {   X_target <- builder_data$X_target   train_center <- colMeans(X_target[train_idx, , drop = FALSE])   source_center <- colMeans(X_train$X)   centered <- sweep(X_target, 2, train_center, "-")   sweep(0.85 * centered, 2, source_center, "+") } builder_design <- feature_sets_design(   X_train = da_example$design_fixed$X_train,   X_test = NULL,   block_var_test = da_example$block_var_test,   target_builder = target_builder,   target_builder_data = list(X_target = da_example$X_rec_noisy),   n_test = nrow(da_example$X_rec_noisy) )  builder_spec <- feature_rsa_da_model(   dataset = da_example$dataset,   design = builder_design,   mode = "coupled",   lambdas = c(low = 0.1, semantic = 0.1),   alpha_target = 0.3,   rho = 3,   rsa_simfun = "spearman" )  builder_res <- run_regional(builder_spec, da_example$region_mask)  builder_res$performance_table[, c("target_rdm_correlation", "target_r2_full")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   target_rdm_correlation target_r2_full #>                    <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1                  0.888          0.788

Use pattern recall single stream rather multi-run design. contiguous folds define target-train target-test segments, target_gap removes rows adjacent held-segment, target_nperm gives single-run null preserves local temporal structure.
code: single_run_spec <- feature_rsa_da_model(   dataset = da_example$dataset,   design = builder_design,   mode = "coupled",   lambdas = c(low = 0.1, semantic = 0.1),   recall_nfolds = 4,   target_gap = 2,   target_nperm = 50 )

Use mode = "coupled" source target mappings similar identical. Use mode = "stacked" want one shared mapping mild state shift. Typical use cases include encoding--recall transfer, perception--imagery transfer, story listening retelling, setting feature space survives change cognitive state.

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: ’ll use small synthetic dataset 80 trials four ROIs. Two model RDMs — say, “category” RDM “feature” RDM: pair_rsa_design() generalization rsa_design(). within-domain case (one item set, lower-triangle pairs) two interchangeable; use pair_rsa_design() keep one consistent design API throughout vignette. Fit RSA model fingerprint capture turned , run regionally. already get standard ROI×metric performance table: new piece per-ROI fingerprint matrix, attached attribute: connectivity summary one line: conn$similarity ROI--ROI representational connectivity model-RDM subspace. Diagonal entries ROI’s model-explained strength; -diagonal entries say much two ROIs share geometry. ROI--ROI model-space connectivity. Diagonal = model-explained strength; -diagonal = shared geometry model RDMs. ’s whole loop. rest vignette unpacks piece can .
code: set.seed(2026) toy <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6, 6, 6), nobs = 80, blocks = 4, nlevels = 2) dset <- toy$dataset nvox <- sum(dset$mask)  # Four arbitrary ROIs over the active voxels. region_mask <- NeuroVol(   sample(seq_len(4), size = nvox, replace = TRUE),   space(dset$mask), indices = which(dset$mask > 0) ) n <- 80L R_cat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4))) R_feat <- as.matrix(stats::dist(matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4))) des <- pair_rsa_design(   items_a = paste0("trial", seq_len(n)),   model   = list(category = R_cat, feature = R_feat) ) m <- rsa_model(   dataset = dset, design = des,   distmethod = "pearson", regtype = "pearson",   return_fingerprint = TRUE )  reg <- run_regional(m, region_mask, verbose = FALSE) reg$performance_table #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   roinum category  feature #>    <int>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1      1 -0.00778  0.00306 #> 2      2  0.0284  -0.00521 #> 3      3  0.0191   0.0155  #> 4      4 -0.0208   0.00748 fp <- attr(reg, "fingerprints") fp$scores #>            PC1          PC2 #> 1  0.007484900  0.003418361 #> 2 -0.023224714 -0.016794183 #> 3 -0.002470507 -0.025059651 #> 4  0.019572108  0.009672595 conn <- model_space_connectivity(reg) round(conn$similarity, 3) #>   1      2     3      4 #> 1 0  0.000 0.000  0.000 #> 2 0  0.001 0.000 -0.001 #> 3 0  0.000 0.001  0.000 #> 4 0 -0.001 0.000  0.000

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: pair_rsa_design() unified pair-coordinate system. supports two modes.

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: classical RSA layout: one item set, lower-triangle pairs. Equivalent rsa_design(): produce length-n*(n-1)/2 pair vectors feed rsa_model() interchangeably. can keep using rsa_design() prefer; pair_rsa_design() design layer can also handle cases .
code: classic <- rsa_design(~ R_cat, list(R_cat = R_cat)) paired  <- pair_rsa_design(   items_a = paste0("trial", seq_len(n)),   model   = list(R_cat = R_cat) )  identical(paired$model_mat$R_cat, classic$model_mat$R_cat) #> [1] TRUE

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: item set item set B differ — example, items shown encoding vs retrieval, items movie vs movie B — natural pair space rectangular n_a × n_b table, lower triangle one square RDM. Used rsa_model(), design causes engine compute rectangular neural pair-dissimilarity block — 1 - cor(rows_A, rows_B) — instead lower triangle. get back per-ROI scores (return_fingerprint = TRUE) per-ROI fingerprints exactly within-domain case. ROI--ROI connectivity cross-domain geometry drops model_space_connectivity() call.
code: n_a <- 6L; n_b <- 8L items_A <- paste0("A", seq_len(n_a)) items_B <- paste0("B", seq_len(n_b))  # Model: a rectangular cross-set RDM (e.g., similarity in a learned embedding) M_cross <- matrix(rnorm(n_a * n_b), n_a, n_b) rownames(M_cross) <- items_A colnames(M_cross) <- items_B  cross_des <- pair_rsa_design(   items_a   = items_A,   items_b   = items_B,   model     = list(M = M_cross),   pairs     = "between",   row_idx_a = seq_len(n_a),                # which dataset rows are in domain A   row_idx_b = n_a + seq_len(n_b)           # which are in domain B )  length(cross_des$model_mat$M)              # n_a * n_b — full rectangular block #> [1] 48

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: Sometimes model RDM easier define function item attributes precomputed matrix: function evaluated pair table; don’t need materialize square matrix.
code: items <- c("apple", "pear", "plum", "fig") features <- data.frame(length = c(5, 4, 4, 3), vowels = c(2, 2, 1, 1))  fdiff <- function(a, b, fa, fb) {   abs(fa$length - fb$length) + abs(fa$vowels - fb$vowels) }  fdes <- pair_rsa_design(   items_a    = items,   features_a = features,   model      = list(feat_distance = fdiff) ) fdes$model_mat$feat_distance #> [1] 1 2 3 1 2 1

title: Model-Space Representational Connectivity
text: Instead materializing full ROI×ROI matrix, cluster per-center fingerprints k-means use searchlight closest centroid anchor. Connectivity becomes n_centers × k similarity matrix plus one brain map per anchor — “voxel’s representational geometry resembles anchor j” map. returned model_space_anchor_connectivity object exposes: Memory O(n_centers × k) regardless model-RDM dimension number trials. already anchor centers mind — example, peak coordinates prior analysis specific parcels — pass seeds = c(<center IDs>) function skips k-means entirely:
code: res_sl <- run_searchlight(m, radius = 6, method = "standard")  conn_sl <- model_space_connectivity(   res_sl,   k = 20,           # number of anchors   scale = "norm",   # cosine similarity   random_seed = 1L  # reproducible k-means starts )  names(conn_sl) conn_sl <- model_space_connectivity(res_sl, seeds = c(101L, 422L, 1037L))

title: Naive Cross-Decoding
text: section documentation covers four related models cross-domain representational analysis. share vocabulary ’s easier keep straight defined : Naive cross-decoding (vignette) — -learning baseline: classify target patterns source prototypes via correlation. Lives naive_xdec_model(). ERA-RSA (vignette("ERA_RSA_Cross_Decoding")) — encoding-retrieval analysis combining first-order item match second-order RDM similarity. Lives era_rsa_model() era_partition_model(). REMAP-RRR (vignette("REMAP_RRR")) — domain-adaptive cross-decoding via reduced-rank regression. Lives remap_rrr_model(). ReNA-Map (vignette("repmap_model"), repmap_model()) — predicts ROI’s pattern low-rank seed feature space. ReNA-RM (vignette("repmed_model"), repmed_model()) — tests whether one ROI’s geometry mediates another’s relationship seed. ReNA-RC (vignette("repnet_model"), repnet_model()) — representational connectivity: correlates ROI’s RDM seed RDM, optional confound RDMs. Reach naive cross-decoding first: ’s cheapest baseline. four answer different questions naive transfer insufficient.

title: Naive Cross-Decoding
text: use cross-decoding data come two related domains, perception memory, want know whether pattern learned source domain transfers target domain. Naive cross-decoding direct-transfer baseline: classifies target-domain patterns correlating source-domain prototypes, learned correction two domains. makes analysis deliberately conservative. works, representations already aligned well enough transfer directly. , result still useful tells much work domain-adaptive model remap_rrr_model() encoding-retrieval partition analysis explain. Algorithm: Compute prototype (mean pattern) category training domain. Correlate every test pattern prototype. Assign category whose prototype highest correlation. Correlations passed softmax produce pseudo-probabilities.

title: Naive Cross-Decoding
text: random data, accuracy hovers around chance. real fMRI data, -chance accuracy indicates category representations transfer across domains.
code: set.seed(42) data_info <- gen_sample_dataset(   D = c(10, 10, 10), nobs = 80, nlevels = 4,   blocks = 4, external_test = TRUE )  # Three ROIs roi_mask <- NeuroVol(   sample(1:3, length(data_info$dataset$mask), replace = TRUE),   space(data_info$dataset$mask) )  model <- naive_xdec_model(   dataset = data_info$dataset,   design  = data_info$design,   return_predictions = TRUE )  print(model) #> Naive Cross-Decoding Model #>   link_by:             (class labels)  #>   training levels:     a, b, c, d  #>   n_train:             80  #>   n_test:              80  #>   return_predictions:  TRUE results <- run_regional(model, roi_mask) metric_cols <- intersect(   c("roinum", "Accuracy", "AUC"),   names(results$performance_table) ) results$performance_table[, metric_cols, drop = FALSE] #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   roinum Accuracy     AUC #>    <int>    <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      1    0.2   -0.0292 #> 2      2    0.238 -0.0562 #> 3      3    0.212  0.0558 chance <- 1 / nlevels(data_info$design$y_test) cat(sprintf("Chance level: %.0f%%\n", chance * 100)) #> Chance level: 25%

what: How does this relate to ERA partitioning?
title: Naive Cross-Decoding
text: Naive cross-decoding answers first-order question: target pattern look like correct source prototype? often first result check maps directly onto classification accuracy. era_partition_model() analysis uses direct-transfer idea one component broader encoding-retrieval analysis. builds matched source target prototypes item key, reports: direct transfer metrics naive_top1_acc xdec_Accuracy, first-order variance partitioning via first_order_delta_r2, second-order geometry preservation via second_order_delta_r2 geom_cor, optional leakage-free Procrustes alignment metrics. Use naive_xdec_model() need clean baseline map ROI table direct transfer. Use era_partition_model() scientific question whether encoding-retrieval transfer reflects item-specific similarity, preserved representational geometry, nuisance structure block, category, temporal lag.

Provide external_test = TRUE gen_sample_dataset(), construct mvpa_dataset() train_data test_data. predictions class — Prototypes may similar. Check inter-prototype correlations; ~0.9, training domain carry enough discriminative information method work. Chance-level performance everywhere — expected outcome large systematic domain shift. title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
text: rMVPA already provides execution harnesses searchlight regional analyses (ROI extraction, iteration, parallel orchestration, spatial map assembly). Plugin analyses let add new method families separate package reusing harness. plugin contract small: Constructor: returns model specification via create_model_spec(). output_schema.<your_class>(): declares metric names layout. fit_roi.<your_class>(): computes one ROI result. Optional strip_dataset.<your_class>(): trims large fields workers.

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
text: different extension paths: Classifier registry (register_mvpa_model()): add new estimator backend (fit/predict/prob) use inside standard mvpa_model analysis. Analysis plugin contract (create_model_spec() + fit_roi.<class>): add new per-ROI analysis type custom logic. Use classifier registry workflow still standard classification/regression estimator changes. Use analysis plugins ROI computation novel.

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
code: toy_plugin_model <- function(dataset, design, center_stat = c("mean", "median")) {   center_stat <- match.arg(center_stat)   create_model_spec(     name = "toy_plugin_model",     dataset = dataset,     design = design,     center_stat = center_stat,     compute_performance = TRUE,     return_predictions = FALSE   ) }

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
code: output_schema.toy_plugin_model <- function(model) {   list(     score = "scalar",     spread = "scalar"   ) }

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
text: classification/regression-style plugins, can delegate fold execution cv_evaluate_roi() instead reimplementing CV loops plugin:
code: fit_roi.toy_plugin_model <- function(model, roi_data, context, ...) {   x <- roi_data$train_data   score <- if (identical(model$center_stat, "median")) stats::median(x) else mean(x)   spread <- stats::sd(as.numeric(x))    roi_result(     metrics = c(score = score, spread = spread),     indices = roi_data$indices,     id = context$id   ) } fit_roi.my_classif_plugin <- function(model, roi_data, context, ...) {   cv_evaluate_roi(     model_spec = model,     roi_data = roi_data,     context = context,     mode = "auto"   ) }

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
text: package, methods discovered automatically package loaded. interactive session, register explicitly:
code: registerS3method(   "fit_roi",   "toy_plugin_model",   fit_roi.toy_plugin_model,   envir = asNamespace("rMVPA") ) registerS3method(   "output_schema",   "toy_plugin_model",   output_schema.toy_plugin_model,   envir = asNamespace("rMVPA") )

what: Fast plugin validation without full pipelines
title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA
text: Use mock_roi_data(), mock_context(), validate_plugin_model() verify contract expensive runs.
code: ds <- gen_sample_dataset(c(4, 4, 4), nobs = 20, nlevels = 2, blocks = 2) mspec <- toy_plugin_model(ds$dataset, ds$design)  plugin_check <- validate_plugin_model(   mspec,   roi_data = mock_roi_data(n_train = 20, n_features = 8, n_test = 0, seed = 1),   context = mock_context(design = ds$design, id = 101L) )  plugin_check #> <plugin_validation_result> class=toy_plugin_model, metrics=2 #>   metric_names: score, spread validate_model_spec(mspec, require_schema = TRUE, dry_run = TRUE) #> <model_spec_validation_result> class=toy_plugin_model valid=TRUE (pass=5 warn=0 fail=0)

title: Building Plugin Analyses for rMVPA

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: Classic cross‑decoding trains patterns one context (e.g., perception) tests another (e.g., memory). two domains rarely match exactly: recall patterns can systematically shifted relative perception. REMAP‑RRR now models memory “perception + low‑rank correction” jointly whitened space: Y_w ≈ X_w + λ X_w Δ, rank(Δ) ≪ p. Key points - Joint shrinkage whitening perception memory prototypes (stable small‑n, large‑p). - Residual reduced‑rank regression (RRR) learns Δ R_w = Y_w − X_w. - Automatic λ selection training items shrinks toward naïve cross‑decoder data weak. - Optional leave‑one‑key‑(LOKO) item’s adaptor diagnostics (e.g., R², improvement naïve) estimated item‑wise cross‑validated fashion. - Correlation classification predicted templates whitened space. LOKO vs single‑fit LOKO (leave_one_key_out = TRUE) primarily unbiased adaptor diagnostics item level. Test‑set classification metrics (accuracy/AUC external test set) always computed trials used fit adaptor, regardless LOKO setting. large searchlight runs adaptor diagnostics secondary speed critical, often reasonable set leave_one_key_out = FALSE use fixed rank, trading slightly optimistic adaptor diagnostics much faster single‑fit adapters per ROI. Note: complete introduction naive cross-decoding (baseline method), see vignette("Naive_Cross_Decoding"). vignette explains algorithm, use cases, domain adaptation needed.

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: simulate dataset external test set (perception→memory) run naive cross‑decoding baseline REMAP‑RRR small ROI mask. Notes - rrpack installed, set rank = 0 (identity adapter) recover naïve cross‑decoding. - Provide link_by per‑item pairing (e.g., "ImageID" train test design tables). NULL, pairing falls back class means.
code:
set.seed(1) toy <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 120, nlevels = 4, blocks = 3, external_test = TRUE)  # Simple region mask: three ROIs with random voxel assignments regionMask <- NeuroVol(sample(1:3, size = length(toy$dataset$mask), replace = TRUE),                        space(toy$dataset$mask))  # 1) Naive baseline: correlate target trials vs. source prototypes ms_naive <- naive_xdec_model(toy$dataset, toy$design, link_by = NULL) res_naive <- run_regional(ms_naive, regionMask)  # 2) REMAP‑RRR: domain‑adaptive mapping into target space ms_remap <- remap_rrr_model(   dataset  = toy$dataset,   design   = toy$design,   link_by  = NULL,       # pair by class labels; provide an item ID column if available   rank     = "auto",     # requires rrpack; set 0 for identity (no adapter)   max_rank = 6,   leave_one_key_out = TRUE,   min_pairs = 5 ) res_remap <- run_regional(ms_remap, regionMask)  cat("Naive regional metrics (first rows):
"); peek(res_naive$performance_table) cat("
REMAP regional metrics (first rows):
"); peek(res_remap$performance_table)

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: regional result includes per‑ROI performance_table usual MVPA metrics new REMAP diagnostics: Standard metrics (classification): Accuracy, AUC (centered −0.5). remap_improv: fraction perception→memory mismatch explained vs naïve (0=gain, 1=perfect). lambda_mean: average λ selected training items (shrink toward naïve). delta_frob_mean: Frobenius norm λ·Δ (size learned correction). adapter_rank, adapter_sv1, adapter_mean_r2 remain completeness. return_adapter = TRUE (default), ROI row also carries rich predictor object inside classification_result containing diagnostic vectors. easiest way inspect call process_roi() single ROI examine result$predictor field. Key fields - r2_per_voxel: predictability target prototypes source (per voxel). - resid_by_item: LOKO residual (MSE) held‑key. - rank_by_item, sv1_by_item: RRR rank leading singular per held‑key. - keys_scored, skipped_keys: transparency LOKO fits. - sv_spectra_by_item: full singular spectra (optional; enable save_fold_singulars = TRUE).
code: # Inspect diagnostics for a single ROI vox <- which(toy$dataset$mask > 0)[1:40] one_roi <- extract_roi(vox, toy$dataset) one_row <- process_roi(ms_remap, one_roi, rnum = 1) pred <- one_row$result[[1]]$predictor str(pred, max.level = 1)

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: Two convenience helpers make easy quantify “transformed memory?” “items closer/farther perception?” Interpretation - mean_roi_improv: higher means adapter explains P→M mismatch. - mean_delta_frob: larger means stronger correction; near 0 indicates “memory ≈ perception”. - res_ratio (item‑level): < 1 means REMAP brings memory closer perception item.
code: # ROI‑level summary (works from performance_table; no fits required) roi_tab <- summarize_remap_roi(res_remap) head(roi_tab)  # Item‑level summary for a specific ROI (needs return_fits = TRUE) res_fit <- run_regional(ms_remap, regionMask, return_fits = TRUE) items_r1 <- summarize_remap_items(res_fit, roi = roi_tab$roinum[1]) head(items_r1)

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: ROI metrics performance_table also available volumetric maps res_remap$vol_results. can write save_results():
code: maps_to_write <- res_remap$vol_results[c("remap_improv", "delta_frob_mean", "lambda_mean")] save_results(maps_to_write, dir = "remap_maps", level = "minimal")

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: Pairing key present designs. Use item IDs want exemplar‑level pairing. Mapping rank. "auto" uses rrpack::cv.rrr; small fixed rank faster. Use 0 disable adaptation (identity mapping). Soft warning fewer min_pairs paired items; hard error < 2. Enables strict per‑item adapter CV avoid leakage; recommended target trials sparse. Store full per‑fold singular spectra (ROIs interest). default keep memory light searchlights. registry classifier works; corclass fast robust small‑n, large‑p.

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: spec works searchlight. brevity show call; running large volumes may time‑consuming. Use small radius /fixed rank quick checks.
code: sl_res <- run_searchlight(ms_remap, radius = 6, method = "randomized", niter = 2) sl_res # Maps include standard metrics and adapter_* summaries. Use names(sl_res$results).

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: “Need least 2 paired items”: adapter requires ≥ 2 paired keys. Reduce min_pairs understand stability trade‑. Warnings like “N paired items (<min_pairs)” informational. Consider fixed small rank rank = 0 baseline small ROIs. LOKO skipped keys reported; check skipped_keys consider pooling items per class using non‑LOKO appropriate.

title: REMAP‑RRR — Domain‑Adaptive Cross‑Decoding
text: Reduced‑rank regression (RRR): implemented via rrpack package. Shrinkage covariance/whitening: corpcor::cov.shrink.

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: hypothesis stimuli relate — category, visual similarity, embedding computational model. RSA tests whether brain’s pattern similarities across stimuli matches predicted pattern. geometry activity, activity , data. whole workflow four steps: () build MVPA dataset, (ii) supply one model RDMs (representational dissimilarity matrices), (iii) wrap rsa_design(), (iv) fit rsa_model() run regionally via searchlight.

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: Build tiny dataset whose first half trials shares one latent structure second half shares another, single model RDM knows . model RDM. Dark cells = predicted small dissimilarity (similar items); bright cells = predicted large dissimilarity. Diagonal = 0. Fit RSA one regional pass: performance_table row reports, per ROI, well ROI’s neural pair-dissimilarity vector tracks model RDM. ’s entire RSA core loop. rest vignette covers parts ’ll actually want control: build model RDMs, combine multiple RDMs, handle correlated RDMs, wire searchlight.
code: set.seed(2026) ds <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(8, 8, 8), nobs = 40, blocks = 4, nlevels = 2)  # A simple ordinal model RDM: nearby trial indices are predicted to be similar. model_rdm <- as.matrix(stats::dist(seq_len(40))) des <- rsa_design(~ model_rdm, list(model_rdm = model_rdm),                   block_var = ds$design$block_var) mod <- rsa_model(ds$dataset, des, distmethod = "pearson", regtype = "pearson") res <- run_regional(mod, region_mask = ds$dataset$mask, verbose = FALSE) res$performance_table #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   roinum model_rdm #>    <int>     <dbl> #> 1      1  -0.00292

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA

code: dataset <- rMVPA::gen_sample_dataset(D = c(20, 20, 8), nobs = 80, blocks = 4)

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: can use different types dissimilarity matrices:
code: # Method 1: Using dist() on feature vectors model_features <- matrix(rnorm(80*10), 80, 10)  # 80 trials, 10 features model_rdm <- dist(model_features)  # Default is Euclidean distance  # Method 2: Direct correlation distance matrix model_matrix <- 1 - cor(t(model_features))  # Correlation distance

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: RSA design specifies compare neural model dissimilarity patterns:
code: # Basic design with one model RDM basic_design <- rsa_design(   formula = ~ model_rdm,   data = list(model_rdm = model_rdm),   block_var = factor(dataset$design$block_var) )  # Design with multiple model RDMs model_rdm2 <- dist(matrix(rnorm(80*10), 80, 10)) complex_design <- rsa_design(   formula = ~ model_rdm + model_rdm2,   data = list(     model_rdm = model_rdm,     model_rdm2 = model_rdm2   ),   block_var = factor(dataset$design$block_var),   keep_intra_run = FALSE  # Exclude within-run comparisons )

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: Sometimes model RDMs independent hypotheses. CNN layer RDMs common example: layers VGG-16 layer 4, 7, 11, 16 ordered often share substantial representational structure. enter RDMs directly multiple-RDM RSA model, shared geometry can make individual layer effects hard interpret. rdm_decorrelate() preprocessing helper situation. works lower-triangular RDM vectors, estimates layer-specific innovation RDMs, returns adjusted RDMs can passed directly rsa_design(). raw RDM correlations show much representational geometry shared across model layers. ordered innovation method treats supplied order meaningful: later-layer RDMs decomposed part predicted earlier layer innovations residual layer-specific component. result records decorrelation achieved much adjusted RDM still resembles original. adjusted RDMs ordinary dist objects, can used standard RSA design. Use want test ordered, related model geometries reducing shared RSA structure among . adjusted RDMs interpreted layer-specific representational innovations, ordinary covariance shrinkage estimates.
code: set.seed(12)  shared_visual <- matrix(rnorm(80 * 4), 80, 4) layer_noise <- lapply(c(0.9, 0.7, 0.5, 0.35), function(sd) {   matrix(rnorm(80 * 4, sd = sd), 80, 4) })  cnn_features <- Map(function(noise, weight) {   base::scale(weight * shared_visual + noise) }, layer_noise, c(0.45, 0.60, 0.75, 0.90)) names(cnn_features) <- c("vgg4", "vgg7", "vgg11", "vgg16")  cnn_rdms <- lapply(cnn_features, dist)  raw_rdm_vectors <- vapply(cnn_rdms, as.vector, numeric(length(cnn_rdms[[1]]))) round(cor(raw_rdm_vectors, method = "spearman"), 2) #>        vgg4  vgg7 vgg11 vgg16 #> vgg4   1.00 -0.01  0.08  0.09 #> vgg7  -0.01  1.00  0.32  0.30 #> vgg11  0.08  0.32  1.00  0.56 #> vgg16  0.09  0.30  0.56  1.00 decorrelated <- rdm_decorrelate(   cnn_rdms,   method = "ordered_innovation",   similarity = "spearman",   epsilon = 0.05,   gamma_grid = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05) )  decorrelated$mean_abs_cor_before #> [1] 0.2258285 decorrelated$mean_abs_cor_after #> [1] 0.04841876 decorrelated$preservation #>      vgg4      vgg7     vgg11     vgg16  #> 1.0000000 1.0000000 0.9479340 0.9067018 cnn_design <- rsa_design(   formula = ~ vgg4 + vgg7 + vgg11 + vgg16,   data = decorrelated$rdms,   block_var = factor(dataset$design$block_var),   keep_intra_run = FALSE )

what: 4. Creating and Running an RSA Model
title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: rsa_model() function supports different methods computing neural dissimilarities analyzing relationships:
code: # Create MVPA dataset dset <- mvpa_dataset(dataset$dataset$train_data, mask=dataset$dataset$mask)  # Create RSA model with different options rsa_spearman <- rsa_model(   dataset = dset,   design = basic_design,   distmethod = "spearman",  # Method for computing neural dissimilarities   regtype = "spearman"      # Method for comparing neural and model RDMs )  # Run searchlight analysis results <- run_searchlight(   rsa_spearman,   radius = 4,   method = "standard" )

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: rMVPA supports several methods comparing neural model RDMs:
code: # Pearson correlation rsa_pearson <- rsa_model(dset, basic_design,                          distmethod = "pearson",                          regtype = "pearson")  # Linear regression rsa_lm <- rsa_model(dset, basic_design,                      distmethod = "spearman",                      regtype = "lm")  # Rank-based regression rsa_rfit <- rsa_model(dset, basic_design,                        distmethod = "spearman",                        regtype = "rfit")

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: keep_intra_run = FALSE parameter tells RSA exclude comparisons patterns within run/block. important : Temporal Autocorrelation: BOLD responses within run temporally autocorrelated Scanner Drift: Within-run patterns may share scanner drift effects Physiological Noise: Within-run patterns may share structured noise breathing, heart rate, etc. ’s visualization keep_intra_run = FALSE : create RSA design keep_intra_run = FALSE: creates RSA design includes ‑run comparisons, excludes within‑run pairs, focuses analysis reliable across‑run similarities.
code:
# Create a small example with 2 runs, 4 trials each mini_data <- matrix(1:8, ncol=1)  # Trial numbers 1-8 run_labels <- c(1,1,1,1, 2,2,2,2)  # Two runs with 4 trials each  # Create distance matrix d <- dist(mini_data) d_mat <- as.matrix(d)  # Show which comparisons are kept (TRUE) or excluded (FALSE) comparison_matrix <- outer(run_labels, run_labels, "!=") # Only show lower triangle to match distance matrix structure comparison_matrix[upper.tri(comparison_matrix)] <- NA  # Display the matrices cat("Trial numbers:
") #> Trial numbers: print(matrix(1:8, nrow=8, ncol=8)[lower.tri(matrix(1:8, 8, 8))]) #>  [1] 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 3 4 5 6 7 8 4 5 6 7 8 5 6 7 8 6 7 8 7 8 8 cat("
Run comparisons (TRUE = across-run, FALSE = within-run):
") #>  #> Run comparisons (TRUE = across-run, FALSE = within-run): print(comparison_matrix[lower.tri(comparison_matrix)]) #>  [1] FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE #> [13]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE #> [25] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE # Create design excluding within-run comparisons blocked_design <- rsa_design(   formula = ~ model_rdm,   data = list(model_rdm = model_rdm),   block_var = factor(dataset$design$block_var),   keep_intra_run = FALSE  # Exclude within-run comparisons )

title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: Set keep_intra_run = FALSE design includes multiple runs want control temporal autocorrelation run‑specific noise; conservative choice confirmatory analyses. Keeping within‑run comparisons (keep_intra_run = TRUE, default) can reasonable short‑run designs, sample size limited, exploratory work maximizing comparisons important strict control temporal structure.

text: rMVPA package provides comprehensive RSA implementation flexible model specification, multiple dissimilarity computation methods, support complex experimental designs run/block structures. integrates seamlessly searchlight analysis offers various statistical approaches including correlation, regression, rank-based methods. using RSA rMVPA, carefully consider experimental design setting block variables intra-run parameters, choose distance methods match theoretical framework, select statistical approaches appropriate analysis goals.

what: From within-ROI scores to ROI-to-ROI connectivity
title: Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) in rMVPA
text: RSA scores per ROI, natural follow-“else geometry live?” rsa_model() accepts return_fingerprint = TRUE, stores small per-ROI projection neural pair vector onto model RDM subspace. model_space_connectivity() turns fingerprints ROI--ROI representational connectivity one call. flag also feeds k-means anchor maps searchlight runs without materialising n_centers × n_centers matrix. See vignette("Model_Space_Connectivity") full workflow, including cross-domain pair designs (pair_rsa_design(..., pairs = "")).

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: Regional MVPA evaluates prediction performance within predefined brain regions. vignette walks complete workflow using rMVPA: ’ll generate synthetic data, define ROIs region mask, build MVPA model cross-validation, run analysis, examine results. implementation follows approach run_regional(), mvpa_model(), mvpa_dataset().

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: begin generating synthetic volumetric dataset using gen_sample_dataset() function. function creates 4D array (spatial dimensions multiple observations), along binary mask associated design cross-validation. returned list contains: dataset: MVPA dataset object training data binary mask. design: MVPA design object specifying response variable block structure.
code: library(rMVPA) library(neuroim2) # Generate a synthetic dataset with dimensions 6x6x6, 80 observations, divided into 4 blocks data_out <- rMVPA::gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 80, blocks = 4, nlevels = 2) print(data_out) #> $dataset #>  #>  MVPA Dataset  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Dimensions:  6 x 6 x 6 x 80 observations  #>   - Type:  DenseNeuroVec  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Mask Information  #>   - Areas:  TRUE : 216  #>   - Active voxels/vertices:  216  #>  #>  #> $design #>  #>  MVPA Design  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Response Type:  Factor #>   - Levels:  a, b  #>   - Class Distribution:  a: 40, b: 40  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Structure  #>   - Blocking:  Present #>   - Number of Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Split Groups:  None

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: regional analysis, need define ROIs. , create region mask randomly assigning active voxel binary mask region label (1 3). simulates scenario brain partitioned three regions interest.
code: # Extract the binary mask from the dataset mask <- data_out$dataset$mask nvox <- sum(mask)  # Create a regional mask: assign each voxel a random region number (1 to 3) set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility region_mask <- neuroim2::NeuroVol(sample(1:3, size = nvox, replace = TRUE), neuroim2::space(mask), indices = which(mask > 0)) table(region_mask) #> region_mask #>  1  2  3  #> 69 74 73

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: Next, create MVPA model evaluate classification task. brief, construct mvpa_dataset, specify design (including block variable response), define model mvpa_model() using chosen classifier cross‑validation strategy. mvpa_model() function, defined mvpa_model.R, packages necessary parameters including cross-validation performance computation.
code: # Create MVPA dataset object from the generated training data and mask  dset <- mvpa_dataset(data_out$dataset$train_data, mask = data_out$dataset$mask)  # Build cross-validation structure using block information from the design cval <- blocked_cross_validation(data_out$design$block_var)  # Load a classification model; here we use "sda" (Shrinkage Discriminant Analysis) mod <- load_model("sda") tune_grid <- data.frame(lambda = 0.01, diagonal = FALSE)  # Create the MVPA model object mvpa_mod <- mvpa_model(mod, dataset = dset, design = data_out$design, crossval = cval, tune_grid = tune_grid) print(mvpa_mod) #> mvpa_model object.  #> model:  sda  #> model type:  classification  #> tune_reps:  15  #> tune_grid:   #>   lambda diagonal #> 1   0.01    FALSE #>  #>  Blocked Cross-Validation  #>  #> - Dataset Information  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Number of Folds:  4  #> - Block Information  #>   - Total Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Block Sizes:  1: 20, 2: 20, 3: 20, 4: 20  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Dataset  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Dimensions:  6 x 6 x 6 x 80 observations  #>   - Type:  DenseNeuroVec  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Mask Information  #>   - Areas:  TRUE : 216  #>   - Active voxels/vertices:  216  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Design  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Response Type:  Factor #>   - Levels:  a, b  #>   - Class Distribution:  a: 40, b: 40  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Structure  #>   - Blocking:  Present #>   - Number of Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Split Groups:  None

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: regional analysis executed run_regional(). Internally prepares ROI indices region mask, applies MVPA model ROI, compiles performance metrics prediction tables. output regional_mvpa_result object performance_table (cross‑validated metrics per region), prediction_table (trial‑level predictions), vol_results (volumetric maps performance across brain).
code: # Run the regional analysis on the defined region mask regional_results <- run_regional(mvpa_mod, region_mask)

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: can inspect performance table evaluate model accuracy region. Cross-validated accuracy per region. dashed line marks chance 2-class problem. bar plot makes -region differences visible glance shows region sits relative chance. detailed view, prediction table shows trial--trial predictions: Volumetric results (vol_results) can visualized neuroimaging tools determine spatial patterns performance.
code: print(regional_results$performance_table) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   roinum Accuracy      AUC #>    <int>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1      1    0.575  0.188   #> 2      2    0.488  0.00125 #> 3      3    0.488 -0.157 # Display the prediction table print(regional_results$prediction_table) #> # A tibble: 240 × 8 #> # Rowwise:  #>    .rownum roinum observed pobserved predicted correct prob_a  prob_b #>      <int>  <int> <fct>        <dbl> <fct>     <lgl>    <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1       1      1 b           0.872  b         TRUE    0.128  0.872   #>  2       2      1 a           0.961  a         TRUE    0.961  0.0393  #>  3       3      1 a           0.996  a         TRUE    0.996  0.00371 #>  4       4      1 b           0.983  b         TRUE    0.0166 0.983   #>  5       5      1 b           0.0243 a         FALSE   0.976  0.0243  #>  6       6      1 a           0.805  a         TRUE    0.805  0.195   #>  7       7      1 b           0.799  b         TRUE    0.201  0.799   #>  8       8      1 a           0.0667 b         FALSE   0.0667 0.933   #>  9       9      1 a           0.999  a         TRUE    0.999  0.00124 #> 10      10      1 a           0.554  a         TRUE    0.554  0.446   #> # ℹ 230 more rows

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: run_regional() function internally calls prep_regional() process region mask, uses mvpa_iterate() apply MVPA model across ROI. Functions combine_regional_results() combine_prediction_tables() merge individual regional outputs comprehensive result. modular design ensures : Data integrity maintained. Cross-validation properly applied. Results aggregated clear interpretation regional level.

title: Advanced Regional MVPA Analysis in rMVPA
text: vignette showed generate synthetic neuroimaging data, define ROIs region masks, set MVPA models cross-validation, run analyses across ROIs, interpret performance metrics. now tools conduct regional MVPA analyses neuroimaging data. details, see vignette("Searchlight_Analysis") searchlight counterpart vignette("CrossValidation") cross-validation strategies.

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: first create small volumetric dataset demonstration. call generates 4D image 6×6×6 spatial voxels 80 observations along time dimension. Observations grouped 4 blocks (20 trials ), response factor y two levels. return value contains data (mvpa_dataset) design (mvpa_design).
code: dataset <- gen_sample_dataset(D=c(6,6,6), nobs = 80, blocks=4, nlevels=2) print(dataset) #> $dataset #>  #>  MVPA Dataset  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Dimensions:  6 x 6 x 6 x 80 observations  #>   - Type:  DenseNeuroVec  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Mask Information  #>   - Areas:  TRUE : 216  #>   - Active voxels/vertices:  216  #>  #>  #> $design #>  #>  MVPA Design  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Response Type:  Factor #>   - Levels:  a, b  #>   - Class Distribution:  a: 40, b: 40  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Structure  #>   - Blocking:  Present #>   - Number of Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Split Groups:  None

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: trials organized runs (blocks), use blocked cross‑validation scheme: train k−1 blocks test held‑block. leave‑one‑group‑strategy respects temporal correlations within runs yields realistic estimate generalization.
code: block <- dataset$design$block_var crossval <- blocked_cross_validation(block) crossval #>  #>  Blocked Cross-Validation  #>  #> - Dataset Information  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Number of Folds:  4  #> - Block Information  #>   - Total Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Block Sizes:  1: 20, 2: 20, 3: 20, 4: 20

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: use shrinkage discriminant analysis model (sda_notune), estimates shrinkage parameter training folds. See sda package details.
code: sda_model <- load_model("sda_notune")  model <- mvpa_model(model=sda_model, dataset=dataset$dataset, design=dataset$design, crossval=crossval) model #> mvpa_model object.  #> model:  sda_notune  #> model type:  classification  #>  #>  Blocked Cross-Validation  #>  #> - Dataset Information  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Number of Folds:  4  #> - Block Information  #>   - Total Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Block Sizes:  1: 20, 2: 20, 3: 20, 4: 20  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Dataset  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Dimensions:  6 x 6 x 6 x 80 observations  #>   - Type:  DenseNeuroVec  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Mask Information  #>   - Areas:  TRUE : 216  #>   - Active voxels/vertices:  216  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Design  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Observations:  80  #>   - Response Type:  Factor #>   - Levels:  a, b  #>   - Class Distribution:  a: 40, b: 40  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Structure  #>   - Blocking:  Present #>   - Number of Blocks:  4  #>   - Mean Block Size:  20  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Split Groups:  None

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: run_searchlight() returns image volumes performance metrics sphere center. two‑class problems report cross‑validated accuracy AUC (centered 0 subtracting 0.5). radius millimeters; method = "standard" evaluates one model per sphere centered voxel.
code: result <- run_searchlight(model, radius=4, method="standard") result

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: randomized variant samples non‑overlapping spheres propagates sphere’s score voxels covers. Repeating niter exhaustive passes yields, voxel, average performance across spheres included . can improve spatial localization often reduces computation, since number models scales nvoxels / radius × niter rather total number voxels.
code: result <- run_searchlight(model, radius=4, method="randomized", niter=8) result

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: classifier rMVPA registry can used searchlight. can also register register_mvpa_model(). Two robust options ship rMVPA hdrda pca_lda:
code: # High‑Dimensional Regularized Discriminant Analysis hdrda <- load_model("hdrda") model_hdrda <- mvpa_model(model = hdrda, dataset = dataset$dataset, design = dataset$design,                           crossval = crossval) res_hdrda <- run_searchlight(model_hdrda, radius = 4, method = "randomized", niter = 2) res_hdrda # PCA + LDA pipeline pca_lda <- load_model("pca_lda") model_pca_lda <- mvpa_model(model = pca_lda, dataset = dataset$dataset, design = dataset$design,                             crossval = crossval) res_pca_lda <- run_searchlight(model_pca_lda, radius = 4, method = "randomized", niter = 2) res_pca_lda

title: Searchlight Analysis
text: REMAP‑RRR models memory perception plus low‑rank correction jointly whitened space. learns residual map Δ prototype residuals (Y_w − X_w) ≈ X_w Δ, builds predicted memory templates Ŷ_w = X_w + λ X_w Δ (λ tuned training items), classifies held‑memory trials correlation Ŷ_w. Provide external test set dataset/design (pattern localizer→WM), optionally link_by column shared train/test designs pair items across domains. Set rank = "auto" positive integer enable reduced‑rank mapping via rrpack.
code: # Synthetic source→target setup toy <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 120, nlevels = 4, blocks = 3, external_test = TRUE) regionMask <- neuroim2::NeuroVol(sample(1:5, size = length(toy$dataset$mask), replace = TRUE),                                  neuroim2::space(toy$dataset$mask))  mspec <- remap_rrr_model(   dataset = toy$dataset,   design  = toy$design,   link_by = NULL,            # defaults to class-wise pairing   rank    = 0,               # identity fallback (no rrpack required)   leave_one_key_out = TRUE ) remap_res <- run_regional(mspec, regionMask) names(remap_res$vol_results)  # includes remap_improv, delta_frob_mean, lambda_mean

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
text: Temporal proximity can induce similarity trial patterns (adaptation, drift, HRF overlap). vignette shows add temporal nuisance RDMs RSA MS-ReVE designs using convenience functions.

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: op <- par(mar=c(3,3,2,1)) image(t(td_plot[nrow(td_plot):1, ]), axes=FALSE, col=cols(64)) box(); title("Temporal RDM (exp kernel)") axis(1, at=c(0,1), labels=c("1","n")); axis(2, at=c(0,1), labels=c("n","1")) invisible(par(op))

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code:
task_rdm <- as.dist(matrix(stats::runif(n*n), n, n)) rtemp <- temporal(onsets, block = runs, kernel = "exp") rdes <- rsa_design(~ task_rdm + rtemp,                    data=list(task_rdm=task_rdm, rtemp=rtemp, onsets=onsets, runs=runs),                    block_var=~ runs, keep_intra_run=TRUE) print(rdes) 
 RSA Design 
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 

Formula:
  |-  ~task_rdm + rtemp 

Variables:
  |- Total Variables:  4 
  |- task_rdm: distance matrix
  |- rtemp: distance matrix
  |- onsets: vector
  |- runs: vector

Structure:
  |- Blocking: Present
  |- Number of Blocks:  3 
  |- Block Sizes:  1: 10, 2: 10, 3: 10 
  |- Comparisons: All included

what: Assessing overlap with a task RDM
title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: task_mat <- as.matrix(task_rdm) tvec <- lower_tri(td_plot) avec <- lower_tri(task_mat) op <- par(mar=c(4,4,2,1)) plot(tvec, avec, pch=16, cex=.6, col="#444444",      xlab="Temporal distance (lower tri)", ylab="Task RDM (lower tri)") abline(lm(avec ~ tvec), col="#cc0000", lwd=2) legend("topleft", bty="n",        legend=sprintf("r = %.2f", stats::cor(tvec, avec))) invisible(par(op))

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: hrf_rdm <- temporal_hrf_overlap(onsets, durations=rep(1, n), run=runs,                                 TR=0.8, similarity="overlap", metric="distance") hrf_mat <- as.matrix(hrf_rdm)  # Stretch dynamic range for plotting (rescale to 0..1 over lower-tri) rng <- range(hrf_mat[lower.tri(hrf_mat)], na.rm = TRUE) hrf_plot <- if (is.finite(diff(rng)) && diff(rng) > 0) (hrf_mat - rng[1]) / diff(rng) else hrf_mat * 0  op <- par(mar=c(3,3,2,1)) image(t(hrf_plot[nrow(hrf_plot):1, ]), axes=FALSE, col=cols(64)) box(); title("HRF overlap RDM (distance)") axis(1, at=c(0,1), labels=c("1","n")); axis(2, at=c(0,1), labels=c("n","1")) invisible(par(op))

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: spec <- list(adj=list(kernel="adjacent", width=1),              exp3=list(kernel="exp", lambda=3),              hrf=list(kind="hrf", TR=0.8)) tc <- temporal_confounds(spec, onsets, run=runs, units="sec", TR=0.8) str(tc) List of 3  $ adj : 'dist' num [1:435] 14 81.5 81.5 81.5 81.5 81.5 81.5 81.5 81.5 0 ...   ..- attr(*, "Size")= int 30   ..- attr(*, "call")= language temporal_rdm(index = onsets, block = run, kernel = item$kernel %||% "exp",      width = item$width %||% 1L, power| __truncated__ ...   ..- attr(*, "method")= chr "temporal:adjacent:distance"  $ exp3: 'dist' num [1:435] 14 39.5 62 81.5 98 ...   ..- attr(*, "Size")= int 30   ..- attr(*, "call")= language temporal_rdm(index = onsets, block = run, kernel = item$kernel %||% "exp",      width = item$width %||% 1L, power| __truncated__ ...   ..- attr(*, "method")= chr "temporal:exp:distance"  $ hrf : 'dist' num [1:435] 27 50.5 72 90 104 115 124 131 135 0 ...   ..- attr(*, "Size")= int 30   ..- attr(*, "call")= language temporal_hrf_overlap(onsets = onsets, durations = item$durations %||% NULL,      run = run, TR = item$TR %||% TR,| __truncated__ ...   ..- attr(*, "method")= chr "temporal_hrf:spm:overlap:distance" op <- par(mfrow=c(1,3), mar=c(3,3,2,1)) for (nm in names(tc)) {   M <- as.matrix(tc[[nm]])   image(t(M[nrow(M):1, ]), axes=FALSE, col=cols(64))   box(); title(paste("confound:", nm)) } invisible(par(op))

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: df <- data.frame(cond = factor(rep(letters[1:3], each=10)), run=runs) mvdes <- mvpa_design(df, y_train=~cond, block_var=~run)  # Single nuisance via kernel Ktemp <- temporal_nuisance_for_msreve(mvpa_design=mvdes,                                       time_idx=seq_len(nrow(df)),                                       kernel="exp", units="index",                                       metric="distance")  # Multiple nuisances from a spec (kernel + HRF) ms_spec <- list(expk=list(kernel="exp", lambda=2), hrf=list(kind="hrf", TR=0.8)) ms_tc <- msreve_temporal_confounds(mvpa_design=mvdes,                                    time_idx=seq_len(nrow(df)), spec=ms_spec) str(ms_tc) List of 2  $ expk: num [1:3, 1:3] 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0  $ hrf : num [1:3, 1:3] 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2   .. ..$ : chr [1:3] "a" "b" "c"   .. ..$ : chr [1:3] "a" "b" "c"

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
text: Prefer within_blocks_only=TRUE trial-level designs unless cross-run timing meaningful. Use rank normalization robust nuisance regressors. HRF overlap relevant events dense durations > 0.

title: Temporal Confounds in RSA
code: # Simulate a toy ROI RDM as a mixture of task + temporal + noise toy_roi <- scale(0.6 * task_mat + 0.3 * td_mat + matrix(rnorm(n*n, sd=.1), n, n)) diag(toy_roi) <- 0; toy_roi <- (toy_roi + t(toy_roi))/2  # Correlation before residualization v_roi  <- lower_tri(toy_roi) v_temp <- tvec cat(sprintf("Before residualization: cor(ROI, temporal) = %.2f\n", stats::cor(v_roi, v_temp))) Before residualization: cor(ROI, temporal) = 0.99 # Residualize ROI distances on temporal distances resid_roi <- stats::lm(v_roi ~ v_temp)$residuals cat(sprintf("After residualization:  cor(resid ROI, temporal) = %.2f\n", stats::cor(resid_roi, v_temp))) After residualization:  cor(resid ROI, temporal) = -0.00 # Visualize effect op <- par(mfrow=c(1,2), mar=c(4,4,2,1)) plot(v_temp, v_roi, pch=16, cex=.6, col="#444444",      xlab="Temporal distance (lower tri)", ylab="ROI distances (lower tri)",      main="Before residualization") abline(lm(v_roi ~ v_temp), col="#cc0000", lwd=2)  plot(v_temp, resid_roi, pch=16, cex=.6, col="#444444",      xlab="Temporal distance (lower tri)", ylab="Residual ROI distances",      main="After residualization") abline(h=0, col="#cc0000", lwd=2) invisible(par(op))

title: Get Started with rMVPA
text: Decoding stimulus categories fMRI activation patterns requires coordinating data loading, model selection, cross-validation, spatial iteration (searchlights ROIs). rMVPA handles can focus scientific question rather pipeline plumbing. vignette walks three analyses synthetic data: Searchlight classification – decode conditions every voxel neighbourhood brain. Regional classification – decode within predefined ROIs. RSA (Representational Similarity Analysis) – test whether neural patterns match predicted similarity structure.

title: Get Started with rMVPA
text: run_searchlight() returns named list performance maps (one per metric). map NeuroVol can write disk neuroim2::write_vol().
code: library(rMVPA) library(neuroim2)  # Generate a synthetic 6 x 6 x 6 dataset with 80 observations in 4 runs data <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6, 6, 6), nobs = 80, blocks = 4,                            nlevels = 2, response_type = "categorical",                            data_mode = "image")  # Build the MVPA model: dataset + design + classifier + cross-validation dset <- mvpa_dataset(data$dataset$train_data, mask = data$dataset$mask) cval <- blocked_cross_validation(data$design$block_var) mod  <- load_model("sda_notune")  mspec <- mvpa_model(mod, dataset = dset, design = data$design,                     crossval = cval,                     tune_grid = data.frame(lambda = 0.01, diagonal = FALSE))  # Run a searchlight with a 4-mm radius sl_result <- run_searchlight(mspec, radius = 4, method = "standard") names(sl_result$results) #> [1] "Accuracy" "AUC"

title: Get Started with rMVPA
text: model spec works ROI-based analysis. just need region mask – integer-labeled volume non-zero value defines ROI. row performance table reports cross-validated metrics one ROI.
code: # Build a toy 3-region mask from the active voxels mask   <- data$dataset$mask nvox   <- sum(mask) set.seed(42) region_mask <- NeuroVol(sample(1:3, nvox, replace = TRUE),                         space(mask), indices = which(mask > 0))  reg_result <- run_regional(mspec, region_mask) print(reg_result$performance_table) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   roinum Accuracy     AUC #>    <int>    <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      1    0.512 -0.0387 #> 2      2    0.512  0.151  #> 3      3    0.462 -0.166

title: Get Started with rMVPA
text: RSA asks whether pattern neural similarities across conditions matches model-predicted similarity structure.
code: # Create a synthetic dataset with 5 conditions rsa_data <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6, 6, 6), nobs = 100, blocks = 5,                                nlevels = 5, response_type = "categorical",                                data_mode = "image")  # Hypothetical model: conditions are ordered, so nearby conditions are similar ncond <- 5 model_rdm <- as.matrix(dist(1:ncond))  # Build the RSA design and model # data= is a list of predictor RDMs; block_var is the run vector rsa_des <- rsa_design(~ model_rdm,                       data = list(model_rdm = model_rdm),                       block_var = rsa_data$design$block_var)  dset_rsa <- mvpa_dataset(rsa_data$dataset$train_data, mask = rsa_data$dataset$mask) rsa_mod  <- rsa_model(dataset = dset_rsa, design = rsa_des,                       distmethod = "spearman", regtype = "pearson")  # Regional RSA rsa_result <- run_regional(rsa_mod, region_mask = rsa_data$dataset$mask) head(rsa_result$performance_table) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   roinum model_rdm #>    <int>     <dbl> #> 1      1    0.0121

Run command line, R Installable wrappers shared workflow flags: vignette("CommandLine") Plug analysis Wrap per-ROI / per-sphere function: vignette("CustomAnalyses") Build full S3 plugin metric schemas: vignette("Plugin_Development")

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: ReNA-Map estimates low-rank linear map seed feature vectors (one vector per item) voxel patterns within ROI/searchlight. summarizes mapping rank, singular values, -sample variance explained (R²) per voxel. Inputs: item key, seed feature matrix (rows = items), fMRI trials grouped item Output (per ROI): map_rank, map_sv1/mean, map_r2_mean/median, map_frob_norm Implementation: reduced-rank regression (rrpack); falls back zero map rrpack installed, warning.

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: Many neuroscience questions ask: information one domain (e.g., semantic features, visual properties, computational model states) encoded neural activity? Traditional encoding models use full-rank regression, can overfit features outnumber observations. ReNA-Map uses reduced-rank regression : Discover intrinsic dimensionality feature→brain mapping (via map_rank) Prevent overfitting constraining mapping low-dimensional subspace Identify dominant encoding axes (via singular values) Example scientific questions: - many independent dimensions semantic space encoded anterior temporal cortex? - V1 use low-rank mapping Gabor filter banks (suggesting sparse coding)? - layers CNN best predict cortex, many principal components needed? Comparison alternatives: - vs. Full regression: ReNA-Map regularizes via rank constraint rather ridge/lasso penalties - vs. RSA: ReNA-Map models item-level patterns directly; RSA models pairwise distances - vs. PLS/CCA: ReNA-Map explicitly estimates coefficient matrix C prediction; PLS focuses latent variable correlation

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: model-derived features (e.g., CNN features, semantic embeddings, computational model states) stimulus item want quantify many dimensions feature space encoded brain region need interpretable mapping coefficients (just correlation strength)

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: design per-trial item key used average trials item-level prototypes. seed feature matrix whose rownames match item IDs design.

what: What does a seed feature space look like?
title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: ReNA-Map needs per-item feature matrix (rows = items, columns = features). toy 6-item, 8-feature seed implied item--item similarity predicts: Left: seed feature matrix (rows = items, columns = features). Right: implied item--item similarity (centered features dot product). ReNA-Map asks ROI many feature dimensions can recover.
code: set.seed(11) K <- 6; P <- 8 items <- paste0("I", seq_len(K)) seedF <- matrix(rnorm(K * P), K, P) rownames(seedF) <- items seed_sim <- tcrossprod(base::scale(seedF))

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: minimal path uses small synthetic dataset single ROI built handful voxels.
code: library(rMVPA)  ## Synthetic dataset ds  <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 60, response_type = "categorical",                           data_mode = "image", blocks = 3, nlevels = 6)  ## Add an item identity column to group trials into item prototypes K <- 6 items <- paste0("I", 1:K) ds$design$train_design$ImageID <- factor(rep(items, length.out = nrow(ds$design$train_design)), levels = items)  ## Seed features (K x P) labeled by item P <- 8 seedF <- matrix(rnorm(K * P), K, P) rownames(seedF) <- items  ## Build design helper and model spec rpdes <- repmap_design(items = items, seed_features = seedF) rpmod <- repmap_model(dataset = ds$dataset, design = ds$design,                       repmap_des = rpdes, key_var = ~ ImageID,                       rank = "auto", max_rank = 5)  ## Define a small ROI (50 voxels) vox <- which(ds$dataset$mask > 0)[1:50] roi <- list(train_roi = neuroim2::series_roi(ds$dataset$train_data, vox), test_roi = NULL)  ## Per-ROI processing out <- process_roi(rpmod, roi, rnum = 1) out$performance[[1]]

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: model can applied across labeled regions. example builds simple two-ROI mask demonstration. step can take longer; keep disabled default.
code: ## Create a toy two-ROI mask by splitting the active mask indices ## (This is just to illustrate the API; use real ROI atlases in practice.) region_vec <- as.vector(ds$dataset$mask) inds <- which(region_vec > 0) half <- length(inds) %/% 2 region_vec[inds[1:half]] <- 1L region_vec[inds[(half+1):length(inds)]] <- 2L region_vec[region_vec <= 0] <- 0L region_mask <- neuroim2::NeuroVol(array(region_vec, dim = dim(ds$dataset$mask)), neuroim2::space(ds$dataset$mask))  ## Run per-ROI mapping (disabled by default to keep vignette fast) # res <- run_regional(rpmod, region_mask) # head(res$performance_table)

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: map_rank: estimated rank (auto via CV fixed). map_sv1 / map_sv_mean: largest mean singular values (mapping strength diagnostics). map_r2_mean / map_r2_median: average/median -sample variance explained across voxels. map_frob_norm: Frobenius norm mapping matrix.

title: Representational Mapping (ReNA-Map): repmap_model
text: Centering: X Y column-centered fitting; standard RRR. Negative R² can occur mapping underperforms mean model; treat diagnostic rather error. Item alignment: items intersected sorted across design, ROI seed features; mismatched labels reduce K. Small K: results flagged potentially unstable K < 5. rrpack missing: method returns zero map (rank=0) logs warning.

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: ReNA-RM tests whether ROI’s representational geometry (mediator M) mediates relationship predictor RDM (X) outcome RDM (Y). reports standard mediation paths (, b, c′), total effect (c), indirect effect (·b), simple Sobel z/p. Inputs: item key, X Y RDMs (K×K), optional confound RDMs Output (per ROI): med_a, med_b, med_cprime, med_c, med_indirect, med_sobel_z/p

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: Key question: brain region actively transform relay information two representational spaces? Standard mediation analysis asks whether M mediates X→Y. Representational mediation extends similarity structures: - X: predictor RDM (e.g., semantic similarity word embeddings) - M: ROI’s neural RDM (computed voxel patterns) - Y: outcome RDM (e.g., behavioral similarity judgments) Example scientific questions: - hippocampus mediate relationship semantic similarity episodic memory confusion? (similar concepts get confused hippocampus encodes similarly?) - inferotemporal cortex transform low-level visual similarity (V1) categorical similarity (perirhinal cortex)? - brain regions along ventral stream actively reshape visual representations vs. passively relay ? Interpretation paths: - Path (X→M): predictor structure correlate neural geometry? (e.g., semantically similar words activate similar patterns?) - Path b (M→Y|X): neural geometry predict outcome similarity controlling predictor? (unique neural contribution) - Path c’ (X→Y|M): Direct effect predictor outcome removing mediation - Indirect effect (×b): much X→Y carried neural geometry? Comparison alternatives: - vs. RSA alone: RSA tests correlation; mediation tests mechanistic pathways - vs. Trial-level mediation: ReNA-RM operates representational geometries (2nd-order), revealing similarity structures transform across regions

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: Trial-level design item key average trials mediator prototypes. X/Y RDMs labeled items; optional labeled confound RDMs.

what: What do the X and Y RDMs look like?
title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: ReNA-RM tests whether region’s geometry mediates predictor RDM X’s relationship outcome RDM Y. toy builds ordinal X RDM noisy linear transformation Y, two related identical. Left: predictor RDM X (clean ordinal). Right: outcome RDM Y (noisy version X). ReNA-RM asks much X -> Y relationship passes ROI’s representational geometry.
code: set.seed(31) K <- 6 items <- paste0("I", seq_len(K)) xi <- matrix(seq_len(K), K, 1) yi <- xi + rnorm(K, sd = 0.1) Xr <- as.matrix(stats::dist(xi)); rownames(Xr) <- colnames(Xr) <- items Yr <- as.matrix(stats::dist(yi)); rownames(Yr) <- colnames(Yr) <- items

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
code: library(rMVPA)  ## Synthetic dataset ds  <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 60, response_type = "categorical",                           data_mode = "image", blocks = 3, nlevels = 6)  K <- 6 items <- paste0("I", 1:K) ds$design$train_design$ImageID <- factor(rep(items, length.out = nrow(ds$design$train_design)), levels = items)  ## Build X and Y RDMs (labeled by items) xi <- matrix(1:K, K, 1) yi <- xi + rnorm(K, 0, 0.1)   # noisy transform Xr <- as.matrix(dist(xi)); rownames(Xr) <- colnames(Xr) <- items Yr <- as.matrix(dist(yi)); rownames(Yr) <- colnames(Yr) <- items  rmdes <- repmed_design(items = items, X_rdm = Xr, Y_rdm = Yr) rmmod <- repmed_model(dataset = ds$dataset, design = ds$design, repmed_des = rmdes,                       key_var = ~ ImageID, distfun = cordist(method = "pearson"))  vox <- which(ds$dataset$mask > 0)[1:50] roi <- list(train_roi = neuroim2::series_roi(ds$dataset$train_data, vox), test_roi = NULL)  out <- process_roi(rmmod, roi, rnum = 1) out$performance[[1]]

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: med_a: association X→M (vectorized RDMs). med_b: association M→Y controlling X. med_cprime: direct effect X→Y controlling M. med_c: total effect X→Y. med_indirect: ·b. med_sobel_z/p: naive Sobel approximation (see caveats).

title: Representational Mediation (ReNA-RM): repmed_model
text: Dependence: entries vectorized RDMs independent; Sobel p-values approximate. inference, prefer permutation/bootstrapping. Label alignment: X/Y/confound RDMs must labeled items; items intersected sorted across sources. Small K: results flagged K < 5.

title: Representational Connectivity (ReNA-RC): repnet_model
text: ReNA-RC quantifies similarity ROI’s item-level representational geometry (RDM) seed RDM. Optionally, nuisance RDMs (confounds) can included, method reports overall similarity regression coefficients. Inputs: item key, seed RDM (K×K), optional confound RDMs (K×K) Output (per ROI): conn_raw (pearson/spearman similarity vectorized RDMs), beta_* multiple regression, optional semipartial correlations

title: Representational Connectivity (ReNA-RC): repnet_model
text: Key question: brain regions share representational geometry seed region computational model? ReNA-RC extension representational similarity analysis (RSA) adds: 1. Confound control: Partial nuisance similarity structures (temporal autocorrelation, low-level visual properties, task structure) 2. Functional connectivity representational space: Like seed-based connectivity, similarity structures rather timecourses 3. Multiple regression framework: Quantify unique variance explained seed vs. confounds Example scientific questions: - regions share hippocampal representational geometry controlling temporal proximity events? - posterior parietal cortex maintain categorical structure prefrontal cortex working memory? - visual areas correlate CNN layer N representations removing retinotopic confounds? Confound control examples: - Temporal lag RDM: Control autocorrelation (nearby trials similar regardless content) - Block RDM: Control slow scanner drift task-set effects - Low-level feature RDM: Isolate high-level representations removing pixel-level similarity confounds matter: Without confound control, might find “connectivity” ’s really just: - regions encode temporal context (content) - regions sensitive low-level features (abstract categories) - regions share task-evoked hemodynamic responses Comparison alternatives: - vs. RSA without confounds: ReNA-RC isolates unique shared geometry - vs. Seed-based connectivity: ReNA-RC measures similarity structure (geometry) rather amplitude covariation - vs. Searchlight RSA: ReNA-RC uses fixed seed (hypothesis-driven) rather whole-brain search

title: Representational Connectivity (ReNA-RC): repnet_model
text: trial-level design item key average trials item prototypes. Seed/Confound RDMs labeled item IDs (rownames/colnames). Unlabeled confounds rejected avoid silent misalignment.

what: What does a seed RDM look like?
title: Representational Connectivity (ReNA-RC): repnet_model
text: seed RDM K x K matrix dissimilarities items. ReNA-RC asks strongly ROI’s representational geometry resembles seed (optionally controlling confounds). toy example uses six-item ordinal seed: items closer index predicted similar. Left: seed RDM (ordinal stack — small dissimilarity near diagonal). Right: confound ‘adjacent’ RDM (1 index-adjacent pairs, 0 otherwise). ReNA-RC measures seed’s variance unique ROI’s geometry, partialling confound.
code: K <- 6 items <- paste0("I", seq_len(K)) S <- as.matrix(stats::dist(seq_len(K))) rownames(S) <- colnames(S) <- items  Adj <- (as.matrix(stats::dist(seq_len(K))) == 1) * 1 rownames(Adj) <- colnames(Adj) <- items

title: Representational Connectivity (ReNA-RC): repnet_model
code: library(rMVPA)  ## Synthetic dataset ds  <- gen_sample_dataset(D = c(6,6,6), nobs = 60, response_type = "categorical",                           data_mode = "image", blocks = 3, nlevels = 6)  K <- 6 items <- paste0("I", 1:K) ds$design$train_design$ImageID <- factor(rep(items, length.out = nrow(ds$design$train_design)), levels = items)  ## Seed RDM (labeled by items) xi <- matrix(1:K, K, 1) S <- as.matrix(dist(xi)) rownames(S) <- colnames(S) <- items  ## Optional confound (e.g., adjacency in item index) Adj <- (as.matrix(dist(xi)) == 1) * 1 rownames(Adj) <- colnames(Adj) <- items  ## Build design helper and model spec rcdes <- repnet_design(design = ds$design, key_var = ~ ImageID, seed_rdm = S,                        confound_rdms = list(adjacent = Adj)) rcmod <- repnet_model(dataset = ds$dataset, design = ds$design, repnet_des = rcdes,                       distfun = cordist(method = "pearson"), simfun = "pearson")  ## Small ROI vox <- which(ds$dataset$mask > 0)[1:50] roi <- list(train_roi = neuroim2::series_roi(ds$dataset$train_data, vox), test_roi = NULL)  out <- process_roi(rcmod, roi, rnum = 1) out$performance[[1]]

title: Contrast RSA Integration Plan for rMVPA
text: Define new S3/S4 class msreve_design. Include slots underlying mvpa_design object. Include slot user-defined contrast_matrix (C, K x Q). Document class purpose. (Optional) Add helper function orthogonalize_contrasts(C) users. Input: mvpa_dataset subset searchlight (sl_data), mvpa_design, cv_spec, estimation_method parameter. cv_spec object generated user functions R/crossval.R (e.g., blocked_cross_validation(mvpa_design$block_var)), defining specific CV strategy. Implement logic compute K x V_sl matrix Û_sl based cross-validation folds defined cv_spec (using helpers like train_indices() get_nfolds()). Support different estimation_methods (e.g., “average_betas”, potentially others later). Add unit tests function. Define new S3 class contrast_rsa_model (inheriting mvpa_model_spec inherits model_spec). Crucially, constructor must use create_model_spec("contrast_rsa_model", ...) internally properly instantiate model object. Takes mvpa_dataset. Takes msreve_design object. Takes parameters: estimation_method, regression_type, output_metric. Add documentation examples constructor. Input: obj (contrast_rsa_model), sl_data, sl_info (containing center voxel index/ID). Step 1: Call compute_crossvalidated_means_sl get Û_sl. Step 2: Compute Ĝ_sl = Û_sl %*% t(Û_sl). Vectorize Ĝ_sl (dvec_sl). Create predictor matrix X_sl obj$design$contrast_matrix. (Reuse/adapt rsa_model_mat logic). Handle block_var exclusions necessary. Step 4: Run regression (using obj$regression_type) get β_sl (Q-vector). Reuse run_lm, run_lm_constrained, etc. Step 5: Compute Δ_sl = t(Û_sl) %*% C (V_sl x Q matrix). Step 6: Extract center voxel’s projection Δ_{v_c,sl} (Q-vector) using sl_info. Step 7: Calculate final result_vector based obj$output_metric (e.g., β_sl * Δ_{v_c,sl}). Step 8: Return named result_vector (Q-dimensional). Add robust error handling within function. Add unit tests method (mocking dependencies).
