title: Constructor for contrast_rsa_model — contrast_rsa_model
code: contrast_rsa_model(   dataset,   design,   estimation_method = "average",   regression_type = "lm",   output_metric = c("beta_delta"),   check_collinearity = FALSE,   normalize_delta = FALSE,   allow_nonorth_composite = FALSE,   calc_reliability = FALSE,   whitening_matrix_W = NULL,   ... )

title: Constructor for contrast_rsa_model — contrast_rsa_model
text: dataset object class mvpa_dataset, containing neuroimaging data associated metadata. design object class msreve_design, containing underlying mvpa_design contrast matrix. Created \link{msreve_design}. estimation_method Character string specifying method estimate cross-validated condition means (U_hat) distances. Supported: "average": Simple mean training samples per condition (U_hat). "L2_norm": Like "average", U_hat rows L2-normalized. "crossnobis": Computes unbiased squared Euclidean distances directly using Crossnobis method. Results distance vector RSA, U_hat matrix empirical G construction way. U_hat delta calculation still computed using "average" method internally selected. Requires `return_folds=TRUE` `compute_crossvalidated_means_sl`. Passed compute_crossvalidated_means_sl ("average", "L2_norm", get per-fold means "crossnobis"). regression_type Character string specifying method RSA regression (regressing empirical RDM/Second Moment Matrix onto contrast RDMs). Options align rsa_model: "pearson", "spearman", "lm", "rfit". Additional options ridge regression: "ridge_hkb" (Hoerl-Kennard-Baldwin lambda). Default "lm". output_metric Character vector specifying one output metrics compute. Multiple metrics can requested simultaneously returned named list. Duplicates removed preserving order first occurrence. Supported options: "beta_delta": product RSA regression coefficient (beta_q)         voxel's projection onto contrast (delta_q,v).         primary signed contribution metric. "beta_only": RSA regression coefficient (beta_q). "delta_only": voxel's projection onto contrast (delta_q,v). "recon_score": Voxel-specific RDM reconstruction score (r_v), correlating RDM implied voxel's loadings empirical RDM. "beta_delta_norm": Similar `beta_delta`, uses L2-normalized voxel contribution vector (delta_q,v). Requires `normalize_delta=TRUE` meaningful. "beta_delta_reliable": Reliability-weighted contributions, \(\rho_{q,v} \beta_q \Delta_{q,v}\), \(\rho_{q,v}\) reflects fold-wise stability. "composite": Sum beta-weighted, L2-normalized voxel contributions (sum q beta_q * delta_q,v). Represents net projection onto positive diagonal contrast space. Interpretation requires caution contrasts orthonormal. Default c("beta_delta"). check_collinearity Logical, whether check collinearity among contrast RDMs using regression_type = "lm". Default FALSE. normalize_delta Logical. TRUE, voxel contribution vector (delta_q,v) normalized unit L2 norm potentially multiplied beta_q "beta_delta" output metric. Default FALSE. allow_nonorth_composite Logical. FALSE (default) composite metric return NA contrast matrix orthonormal, avoid mis-interpretation. TRUE, composite score returned regardless, warning still emitted. calc_reliability Logical. TRUE, voxel-wise contribution reliability (rho values) estimated across cross-validation folds training can incorporated output metrics. Default FALSE. whitening_matrix_W Optional V x V numeric matrix (voxels x voxels). Required `estimation_method = "crossnobis"` Mahalanobis (rather Euclidean) distances desired. matrix (e.g., \(\Sigma_{noise}^{-1/2}\)) passed `compute_crossvalidated_means_sl` whiten per-fold estimates distance calculation. Default `NULL` (Euclidean distances Crossnobis). ... Additional arguments passed create_model_spec.

title: Constructor for contrast_rsa_model — contrast_rsa_model
text: object class contrast_rsa_model, mvpa_model_spec,   model_spec, list.

title: Constructor for contrast_rsa_model — contrast_rsa_model
text: model designed MS-ReVE style analyses goal understand different predefined contrasts contribute representational structure observed neural data, particularly fine-grained (e.g., voxel) level. involves: 1. Estimating cross-validated condition mean patterns (U_hat) distances (Crossnobis). 2. Constructing empirical second-moment matrix (G_hat) U_hat using Crossnobis distances directly. 3. Creating theoretical second-moment matrices (RDMs) contrast vector. 4. Regressing vectorized empirical RDM/distances onto vectorized contrast RDMs get beta coefficients. 5. Projecting voxel patterns (U_hat) onto contrast space get delta values. 6. Combining beta delta form final output metric (e.g., beta_delta). **Cross-Validation Compatibility:** `estimation_method` relies `compute_crossvalidated_means_sl` , turn, requires cross-validation specification (`cv_spec` derived `mvpa_design$crossval`) provide deterministic, partition-based set training indices fold. Therefore, cross-validation schemes like `bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation` (use resampling replacement training folds) **suitable** use model, align assumptions `compute_crossvalidated_means_sl`. Schemes like `blocked_cross_validation`, `kfold_cross_validation`, `custom_cross_validation` (define clear partitions) appropriate. `twofold_blocked_cross_validation` `sequential_blocked_cross_validation`, compatibility also depends whether `train_indices` methods can deterministically define training sets fold iterated `compute_crossvalidated_means_sl`.

title: Constructor for contrast_rsa_model — contrast_rsa_model
code: # --- Minimal Setup --- # 1. Create dummy data and an mvpa_dataset    # Dummy data: 16 samples, 10 voxels, 4 conditions, 2 runs   set.seed(123)   n_samples <- 16   n_voxels <- 10   n_conditions <- 4 # condA, condB, condC, condD   n_runs <- 2    dummy_array <- array(rnorm(n_voxels * n_samples), c(n_voxels, 1, 1, n_samples))   dummy_space <- neuroim2::NeuroSpace(c(n_voxels, 1, 1, n_samples))   dummy_sl_vec <- neuroim2::NeuroVec(dummy_array, dummy_space)    dummy_mask <- neuroim2::NeuroVol(     array(1, c(n_voxels, 1, 1)),     neuroim2::NeuroSpace(c(n_voxels, 1, 1))   )    condition_labels <- factor(     rep(paste0("cond", LETTERS[1:n_conditions]),         each = n_samples / n_conditions)   )   run_labels <- factor(rep(1:n_runs, each = n_samples / n_runs))    # Create mvpa_dataset (without Y and block_var)   mvpa_dat <- rMVPA::mvpa_dataset(     train_data = dummy_sl_vec,     mask = dummy_mask   )    # Create mvpa_design   mvpa_des <- rMVPA::mvpa_design(     train_design = data.frame(condition = condition_labels, run = run_labels),     y_train = ~condition,     block_var = ~run   )    condition_levels <- levels(rMVPA::y_train(mvpa_des))   K <- length(condition_levels)    C_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = K, ncol = 2)   rownames(C_mat) <- condition_levels   C_mat["condA", 1] <- 1; C_mat["condB", 1] <- 1   C_mat["condC", 1] <- -1; C_mat["condD", 1] <- -1   C_mat["condA", 2] <- 1; C_mat["condB", 2] <- -1   C_mat <- base::scale(C_mat, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)   colnames(C_mat) <- c("AB_vs_CD", "A_vs_B")    msreve_des <- rMVPA::msreve_design(     mvpa_design = mvpa_des, # Use mvpa_des here     contrast_matrix = C_mat   )    # --- Example 1: Basic contrast_rsa_model ---   model_basic <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des   )   print(model_basic) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  average  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       beta_delta  #>     # --- Example 1b: Requesting multiple metrics ---   model_multi_metric <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des,     output_metric = c("beta_delta", "recon_score", "beta_only")   )   print(model_multi_metric) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  average  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       beta_delta, recon_score, beta_only  #>     # --- Example 2: Using L2_norm for U_hat and normalize_delta ---   model_l2_norm_delta <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des,     estimation_method = "L2_norm",     normalize_delta = TRUE,     output_metric = "beta_delta_norm"   )   print(model_l2_norm_delta) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  L2_norm  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       beta_delta_norm  #>     # --- Example 3: Ridge Regression (HKB) ---   model_ridge <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des,     regression_type = "ridge_hkb"   )   print(model_ridge) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  average  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  ridge_hkb  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       beta_delta  #>     # --- Example 4: Reconstruction Score Output ---   model_recon <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des,     output_metric = "recon_score"   )   print(model_recon) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  average  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       recon_score  #>     # --- Example 5: Composite Score Output ---   C_mat_ortho <- rMVPA::orthogonalize_contrasts(C_mat)   msreve_des_ortho <- rMVPA::msreve_design(       mvpa_design = mvpa_des, # Use mvpa_des here       contrast_matrix = C_mat_ortho   ) #> Warning: Contrast matrix has no rownames and mvpa_design$conditions is NULL. Assuming row order matches sorted unique condition labels from Y for block mapping.   print(paste("Is contrast matrix orthonormal:", attr(msreve_des_ortho, "is_orthonormal"))) #> [1] "Is contrast matrix orthonormal: TRUE"    model_composite <- contrast_rsa_model(     dataset = mvpa_dat,     design = msreve_des_ortho,     output_metric = "composite",     normalize_delta = TRUE    )   print(model_composite) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  average  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       composite  #>     # --- Example 6: Crossnobis estimation_method ---   # This only shows setting the method. Actual training would require passing   # a pre-computed whitening_matrix_W to compute_crossvalidated_means_sl,   # which is called by train_model.contrast_rsa_model.   model_crossnobis <- contrast_rsa_model(       dataset = mvpa_dat,       design = msreve_des,       estimation_method = "crossnobis"   )   print(model_crossnobis) #>  #>  Contrast RSA Model Specification  #>  #> - Dataset  #>   - Class:  mvpa_image_dataset  #> - Design (`msreve_design`)  #>   - Name:  msreve_design_01  #>   - Contrast Matrix Dims:  4 Cond x 2 Contrasts  #>   - MVPA Design Class:  mvpa_design  #> - Analysis Parameters  #>   - Estimation Method (Uhat/d):  crossnobis  #>   - Whitening Matrix (W):  None (for Euclidean)  #>   - Regression Type (beta):  lm  #>   - Calc Reliability:      FALSE  #>   - Output Metric(s):       beta_delta  #>

Options: `"none"` (default), `"sd"` (divide sample standard deviation), `"l2"` (divide L2 norm / vector length get unit vectors). argument *ignored* `orth = TRUE`. orth Logical. FALSE (default), matrix columns represent specified contrasts directly (centering/scaling). TRUE, orthonormal basis column space computed via QR decomposition. Resulting columns orthogonal unit length (L2 norm = 1). keep_attr Logical. TRUE (default) `orth = TRUE`, original column names (orthogonalization) stored `attr(C, "source")`.

title: Generate Contrast Matrices — contrasts
code: labs <- c("faces","animals","plants","tools",           "vehicles","furniture","buildings","food")  # 1) Mini-DSL: 2x2 Factorial (Animacy x Size) + Interaction, Orthonormal C1 <- contrasts(         labels = labs,         spec   = ~ anim( faces + animals + plants + food ~ . )                  + size( faces + animals + tools + furniture ~ . )                  + anim:size,         orth   = TRUE) print(colnames(C1)) #> [1] "Orth1" "Orth2" "Orth3" print(attr(C1, "source")) #> [1] "anim"      "size"      "anim:size" print(round(crossprod(C1), 5)) #>       Orth1 Orth2 Orth3 #> Orth1     1     0     0 #> Orth2     0     1     0 #> Orth3     0     0     1  # 2) Mini-DSL: One-vs-rest, Centered, Unit Length (L2) C2 <- contrasts(labels = labs,                 spec   = ~ faces( faces ~ . ) + tools( tools ~ . ),                 scale = "l2") print(round(colSums(C2^2), 5)) # Should be 1 #> faces tools  #>     1     1   # 3) Metadata + Formula: Centered, Scaled (SD) meta <- tibble::tribble(   ~label,      ~anim, ~size,   "faces",        1,    0,   "animals",      1,    0,   "plants",       1,    1,   "tools",        0,    0,   "vehicles",     0,    1,   "furniture",    0,    0,   "buildings",    0,    1,   "food",         1,    1) # Note: labels argument is ignored here, order comes from meta$label # Also note: This function masks stats::contrasts C3 <- contrasts(metadata = meta,                 spec     = ~ anim + size + anim:size,                 scale    = "sd") print(round(colMeans(C3), 5)) # Should be 0 #>      anim      size anim:size  #>         0         0         0  print(round(apply(C3, 2, sd), 5)) # Should be 1 #>      anim      size anim:size  #>         1         1         1

code: block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50) weights <- runif(length(block_var)) weights[1] = 0 cval <- bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, weights=weights) X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10) y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))  sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y) block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50) cval <- blocked_cross_validation(block_var) X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10) y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))  sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y) block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50) nfolds <- 2 nreps <- 4 cval <- sequential_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, nfolds, nreps) X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10) y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))  sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y) sample_set <- list(   list(train = 1:80, test = 81:100),   list(train = 1:60, test = 61:100),   list(train = 1:40, test = 41:100) ) cval <- custom_cross_validation(sample_set) X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 10), 100, 10) y <- rep(letters[1:4], length.out=100)  sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y)

title: Distance Function Constructors — cordist
code: cordist(   labels = NULL,   method = c("pearson", "spearman"),   center = c("none", "stimulus_mean") )  mahadist(labels = NULL, center = c("none", "stimulus_mean"))  eucdist(labels = NULL, center = c("none", "stimulus_mean"))  euclidean(labels = NULL, center = c("none", "stimulus_mean"))  robustmahadist(labels = NULL, center = c("none", "stimulus_mean"))  pcadist(   labels = NULL,   ncomp = 2,   whiten = TRUE,   threshfun = NULL,   center = c("none", "stimulus_mean"),   dist_method = c("euclidean", "manhattan", "cosine") )

title: Distance Function Constructors — cordist
text: labels Optional vector row labels (directly used distance calculation). method cordist, correlation method: "pearson" "spearman". center Optional pattern-centering method applied X distances computed. Use "stimulus_mean" subtract across-stimulus mean pattern (Hanson-style). ncomp pcadist, number components (function threshold). whiten pcadist, whether whiten principal components (logical). threshfun pcadist, optional function determines many PCs retain based pres$sdev^2. dist_method pcadist, base distance measure PC space ("euclidean", "manhattan", "cosine").

title: Distance Function Constructors — cordist
text: S3 object class c("<method>", "distfun").

title: Distance Function Constructors — cordist
text: - cordist(labels, method="pearson") -> correlation-based distance. - mahadist(labels) -> Mahalanobis distance. - eucdist(labels) -> Euclidean distance. - robustmahadist(labels) -> Mahalanobis distance using robust covariance. - pcadist(labels, ...) -> distance reduced PCA space.

title: Distance Function Constructors — cordist
code: dist_obj_1 <- cordist(method="pearson") dist_obj_2 <- mahadist() dist_obj_3 <- eucdist() dist_obj_4 <- robustmahadist() dist_obj_5 <- pcadist(ncomp=2, dist_method="cosine")

title: ERA Variance-Partition Model — era_partition_model
text: Compares first-order encoding-retrieval transfer second-order geometry preservation using matched variance-partition models. model builds item-level prototypes source state (`dataset$train_data`) target state (`dataset$test_data`), estimates much variance uniquely explained -item transfer source-state geometry optional nuisance pair models.

title: ERA Variance-Partition Model — era_partition_model
text: dataset `mvpa_dataset` `train_data` `test_data`. design `mvpa_design` train/test design tables. key_var Column name formula giving item key shared across source target states. distfun Distance function used within-state RDMs. rsa_simfun Correlation method raw geometry summary. first_order_nuisance Optional named list `K x K` matrices length `K^2` vectors cross-state similarity nuisance regressors. Matrices interpreted target rows source columns. second_order_nuisance Optional named list `K x K` matrices lower-triangle vectors geometry nuisance regressors. item_block_enc, item_block_ret Optional item-level block labels source target states. Named vectors matched item keys. item_time_enc, item_time_ret Optional item-level time/order values source target states. Named vectors matched item keys. item_category Optional item-level category labels used add -category nuisance model first- second-order regressions. compute_xdec_performance Logical; compute trial-level naive cross-decoding performance using prototype scorer naive_xdec_model. xdec_link_by Optional column name used define source/target labels trial-level cross-decoding metrics. NULL, key_var used. include_procrustes Logical; compute leave-one-item-orthogonal Procrustes cross-decoding metrics. procrustes_center Logical; center source target prototypes using alignment-training items fitting Procrustes maps. min_procrustes_train_items Minimum number paired items allowed leave-one-item-Procrustes alignment. return_matrices Logical; store prototype/similarity matrices ROI result diagnostics. return_xdec_predictions Logical; store trial-level classification_result produced direct cross-decoder ROI result. ... Additional fields stored model spec.

what: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
text: Combines first-order encoding-retrieval similarity (ERA) second-order RSA encoding retrieval representational geometries. Works run_regional() run_searchlight() using standard rMVPA iterators.

title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
code: era_rsa_model(   dataset,   design,   key_var,   phase_var,   encoding_level = NULL,   retrieval_level = NULL,   distfun = cordist(method = "pearson"),   rsa_simfun = c("pearson", "spearman"),   confound_rdms = NULL,   include_diag = TRUE,   item_block = NULL,   item_lag = NULL,   item_run_enc = NULL,   item_run_ret = NULL,   ... )

title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
text: dataset mvpa_dataset train_data (encoding) test_data (retrieval). design mvpa_design describing trial structure train/test designs. key_var Column name formula giving item key links encoding retrieval trials (e.g., ~ ImageID). phase_var Column name formula giving phase labels (must include encoding retrieval levels using single-phase dataset; default two-dataset usage, still parsed consistency required operations). encoding_level Level phase_var treat encoding (default: first level). retrieval_level Level phase_var treat retrieval (default: second level). distfun distfun used compute within-phase RDMs (e.g., cordist("pearson")). rsa_simfun Character: similarity comparing RDMs, one "pearson" "spearman". confound_rdms Optional named list KxK matrices "dist" objects encoding item-level nuisance/model RDMs (e.g., block, run, time). Rows columns correspond item keys (levels key_var). run_enc run_ret entries present used compute geom_cor_run_partial, ER geometry correlation regressing run confounds. include_diag Logical; TRUE (default) ERA -diagonal mean excludes diagonal setting NA first; diagonal metrics always retained. item_block Optional factor per-item blocks, aligned item keys. Typically derived aggregating trial-level block/run variable design$train_design item level (e.g., modal block per item). Used compute block-limited ERA contrasts (era_diag_minus_off_same_block / era_diag_minus_off_diff_block). item_lag Optional numeric per-item retrieval-minus-encoding lag, aligned item keys. Often derived trial onsets (e.g., mean retrieval onset minus mean encoding onset per item). Used compute era_lag_cor, correlation ERA diagonal lag. item_run_enc Optional factor per-item encoding runs, aligned item keys (e.g., modal run encoding trials item). Combined item_run_ret compute geom_cor_xrun, ER geometry restricted item pairs differing encoding retrieval run. item_run_ret Optional factor per-item retrieval runs, aligned item keys. See item_run_enc used. ... Additional fields stored model spec.

title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
text: model spec class "era_rsa_model" compatible run_regional()/run_searchlight().

title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
text: Key outputs per ROI/searchlight sphere include: - First-order ERA: top-1 accuracy, diagonal mean, diagonal-minus-. - Second-order geometry: correlation encoding retrieval RDMs. - Optional confound-aware metrics diagnostics (block/lag/run).

title: ERA-RSA: Encoding-Retrieval Similarity and ER Geometry — era_rsa_model
text: ROI / searchlight center, era_rsa_model emits set scalar metrics turned spatial maps run_regional() run_searchlight(): n_items Number unique item keys contributing ROI/sphere     (.e., length common encoding-retrieval item set). era_top1_acc Top-1 encoding\(\rightarrow\)retrieval accuracy item level:     fraction retrieval trials whose similar encoding pattern     (items) key_var. era_diag_mean Mean encoding-retrieval similarity matching items     (mean diagonal encoding\(\times\)retrieval similarity     matrix). era_diag_minus_off Diagonal-minus--diagonal ERA contrast: era_diag_mean minus     mean similarity non-matching items, capturing much     -item pairs stand pairs. geom_cor Correlation encoding retrieval representational geometries:     correlation (Pearson Spearman, per rsa_simfun)     vectorised lower triangles encoding retrieval RDMs. era_diag_minus_off_same_block Block-limited ERA contrast item_block supplied:     diagonal ERA minus mean similarity items     block (e.g., run/condition), averaged items. era_diag_minus_off_diff_block Cross-block ERA contrast item_block supplied:     diagonal ERA minus mean similarity items different blocks. era_lag_cor Lag-ERA correlation item_lag supplied: Spearman     correlation diagonal ERA values per-item lag     (e.g., retrieval minus encoding onset), using complete cases . geom_cor_run_partial Run-partial ER geometry correlation, run-level confounds     supplied via confound_rdms$run_enc confound_rdms$run_ret.     Computed correlation encoding retrieval RDMs     regressing run RDMs. geom_cor_xrun Cross-run-ER geometry correlation, item_run_enc     item_run_ret supplied: correlation encoding     retrieval RDMs restricted item pairs differ encoding     retrieval run. beta_* confound_rdms provided, additional beta_<name>     terms give regression coefficients linear model predicting     retrieval geometry encoding geometry confound RDMs     (one coefficient per nuisance/model RDM). sp_* run_lm_semipartial() available, sp_<name> terms     provide semi-partial R\(^2\)-like diagnostics confound RDM,     quantifying unique variance explained retrieval geometry.

F feature space matrix (observations features). supplied, overrides S k. labels Vector labels corresponding rows/columns S observations F. k Integer specifying number feature dimensions retain using S. 0 (default), automatically determines dimensions using eigenvalue threshold > 1 (minimum 2 dimensions kept). parameter ignored F supplied directly (k becomes ncol(F)). max_comps Initial upper limit number components derived feature space F subsequent `feature_rsa_model` methods (PCA, PLS). value automatically capped final feature dimensionality `k`. Default 10. block_var Optional blocking variable cross-validation. provided `crossval` `NULL` `feature_rsa_model`, blocked cross-validation scheme generated using vector.

title: Create a Feature-Based RSA Design — feature_rsa_design
text: function defines feature space representation analysis. F supplied directly, used -, `k` becomes `ncol(F)`. S supplied, eigen decomposition S performed. `k` determines many eigenvectors form feature matrix F. `k=0`, dimensions eigenvalues > 1 kept (minimum 2). `max_comps` sets upper bound number components model-fitting methods (like PCA, PLS `feature_rsa_model`) can use, exceed final feature dimensionality `k`.

text: Creates model feature-based Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) relates neural patterns (X) predefined feature space (F).

title: Create a Feature-Based RSA Model — feature_rsa_model
code: feature_rsa_model(   dataset,   design,   method = c("pls", "pca", "glmnet"),   crossval = NULL,   ncomp_selection = c("loo", "max", "pve"),   pve_threshold = 0.9,   alpha = 0.5,   cv_glmnet = FALSE,   lambda = NULL,   nperm = 0,   permute_by = c("features", "observations"),   save_distributions = FALSE,   return_rdm_vectors = FALSE,   ... )

title: Create a Feature-Based RSA Model — feature_rsa_model
text: dataset mvpa_dataset object containing neural data (X). design feature_rsa_design object specifying feature space (F) including component limit (`max_comps`). method Character string specifying analysis method. One : pls Partial Least Squares regression predicting X F (via pls::plsr). pca Principal Component Regression predicting X PCs F (via pls::pcr). glmnet Elastic net regression predicting X F using glmnet multivariate Gaussian response. crossval Optional cross-validation specification. ncomp_selection Character string controlling number components chosen pls pca methods.  One : loo (Default) Fit leave-one-validation select     fewest components within one standard error minimum RMSEP     (pls::selectNcomp, method "onesigma"). pve Keep fewest components whose cumulative explained     variance reaches pve_threshold total explained     fitted components. max Use max_comps components (legacy behaviour). Ignored method = "glmnet". pve_threshold Numeric (0, 1].  ncomp_selection = "pve", proportion total explained X-variance stop adding components.  Default 0.9. alpha Numeric value 0 1, used method="glmnet". Controls elastic net mixing parameter: 1 lasso (default), 0 ridge, values mixture. Defaults 0.5 (equal mix ridge lasso). cv_glmnet Logical, TRUE method="glmnet", use cv.glmnet automatically select optimal lambda value via cross-validation. Defaults FALSE. lambda Optional numeric value sequence values, used method="glmnet" cv_glmnet=FALSE. Specifies regularization parameter. NULL (default), sequence automatically determined glmnet. nperm Integer, number permutations run statistical testing model performance metrics merging cross-validation folds. Default 0 (permutation testing). permute_by DEPRECATED. Permutation always done shuffling rows predicted matrix. save_distributions Logical, TRUE nperm > 0, save full null distributions permutation test. Defaults FALSE. return_rdm_vectors Logical; TRUE, retain ROI's predicted lower-triangle RDM vector regional result's `fits` slot. default can add substantial memory use long time series many ROIs. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). Passing deprecated arguments cache_pca now results error.

title: Create a Feature-Based RSA Model — feature_rsa_model
text: feature_rsa_model object (S3 class).

title: Create a Feature-Based RSA Model — feature_rsa_model
text: Feature RSA models analyze well feature matrix F (defined `design`) relates neural data X. `max_comps` parameter, inherited `design` object, sets upper limit number components fitted:   - pls: PLS regression via pls::plsr. Fits `max_comps` components;     actual number used prediction chosen ncomp_selection.   - pca: Principal Component Regression via pls::pcr. Fits     `max_comps` components; selection controlled ncomp_selection.   - glmnet: Elastic net regression via glmnet multivariate Gaussian     response. Regularisation (lambda) can auto-selected via cv_glmnet=TRUE. pls pca, ncomp_selection argument determines many fitted components actually used prediction.  default ("loo") fits model leave-one-cross-validation picks fewest components within one SE minimum RMSEP. **Performance Metrics** (computed `evaluate_model` cross-validation): *Condition-pattern metrics* (trial x trial correlation matrix):   - `pattern_correlation`: Average correlation predicted observed     spatial patterns corresponding trials (diagonal trial x trial     correlation matrix computed across voxels).   - `pattern_discrimination`: `pattern_correlation` minus mean -diagonal     correlation.  Measures much better model predicts correct trial's     pattern compared incorrect trials.   - `pattern_rank_percentile`: trial, percentile rank correct     pattern match among candidates.  0.5 = chance, 1 = perfect. *Representational geometry*:   - `rdm_correlation`: Spearman correlation upper triangles     observed predicted RDMs (defined 1 - trial--trial correlation     across voxels).  Captures similarity representational geometry. *Global reconstruction metrics*:   - `voxel_correlation`: Correlation flattened predicted observed     matrices (trials x voxels).   - `mse`: Mean Squared Error.   - `r_squared`: 1 - RSS/TSS. *Voxel encoding fidelity*:   - `mean_voxelwise_temporal_cor`: Average per-voxel temporal correlation     predicted observed time courses. - `p_*`, `z_*`: `nperm > 0`, permutation-based p-values z-scores     metrics. number components actually used (`ncomp`) region/searchlight also included performance output.

code: # \donttest{   S <- as.matrix(dist(matrix(rnorm(5*3), 5, 3)))   labels <- factor(letters[1:5])   des <- feature_rsa_design(S = S, labels = labels)   # mdl <- feature_rsa_model(dataset, des, method="pls") # }

what: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: Many rMVPA analyses use continuous predictors (rows = TRs/observations, columns = stimulus features) explain continuous neural responses (rows = TRs, columns = voxels/parcels). practice, stimulus predictors often come multiple correlated blocks (e.g. VGG low/mid/high/semantic PCs, audio vs vision features), useful : regularize block differently (banded/grouped ridge), compute attribution/competition measures leave-one-set-\(\Delta R^2\). Wrap predictor representation `feature_sets` instance.

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
code: feature_sets(x, spec = NULL, set_order = NULL, row_weights = NULL)

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: x Either (1) numeric matrix (observations x features) set spec, (2) named list numeric matrices, one per set (number rows), column-bound `set_order`. spec set spec created `blocks()` `by_set()` (required `x` matrix). set_order Optional character vector giving desired set order (defaults order implied `spec` names list). row_weights Optional numeric vector length nrow(X), used observation weights downstream models (default: 1).

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: object class `feature_sets`.

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: `feature_sets()` constructor wraps predictor matrix (list per-set matrices) `feature_sets` object carries set labels column indices downstream models need manually track slices. can supply predictors two equivalent ways: single matrix X (observations x features) plus set specification         via `blocks()` `by_set()`. named list matrices, one per set, number rows.         matrices column-bound `set_order`. returned object includes: `X`: concatenated numeric predictor matrix, `set`: factor length `ncol(X)` giving per-column set membership, `indices`: named list mapping set name -> integer column indices, `dims`: number columns per set, `row_weights`: optional observation weights (length `nrow(X)`).

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: Use `blocks()` columns already concatenated consecutive blocks (e.g. [low|mid|high|sem]). Use `by_set()` per-column set label vector (non-contiguous groups).

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
text: `feature_sets` object also stores `row_weights`. optional observation weights (length = nrow(X)). primarily intended soft-alignment use cases recall predictors computed gamma %*% X_enc remaining posterior mass can used -weight uncertain recall TRs (see `expected_features()`).

title: Feature sets: grouped predictor matrices — feature_sets
code: X <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 8), 20, 8) fs <- feature_sets(X, blocks(low = 3, sem = 5)) fs #> feature_sets #> =========== #>  #> Observations: 20 #> Features:     8 #> Sets:         2 (low, sem) # 1) Matrix input + blocks() X <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 8), 20, 8) fs <- feature_sets(X, blocks(low = 3, sem = 5))  # 2) List input (already split per set) Xlist <- list(low = X[, 1:3], sem = X[, 4:8]) fs2 <- feature_sets(Xlist)

title: Feature-sets design (mvpa_design extension for continuous regression) — feature_sets_design
text: X_train `feature_sets` object (train/source predictors). X_test Optional `feature_sets` object (test/target predictors). block_var_test Optional integer/factor vector length T_rec defining test/target blocks (typically runs). Used models test-time CV. target_builder Optional function rebuilds target-domain predictors per outer target fold. called named arguments including `X_train`, `X_test`, `train_idx`, `test_idx`, `fold_id`, `block_var_test`, `n_test`, `builder_data`, `design`; callbacks may accept subset need. return value must resolve target predictors feature/set layout `X_train`. target_builder_data Optional object passed `target_builder` `builder_data`. n_test Optional target-domain row count used `X_test` supplied. omitted, inferred `X_test` `block_var_test`. split_by Optional split definition passed `mvpa_design`.

title: Feature-sets design (mvpa_design extension for continuous regression) — feature_sets_design
text: object inheriting `mvpa_design` class `feature_sets_design`.

title: Feature-sets design (mvpa_design extension for continuous regression) — feature_sets_design
text: mirrors pattern used archived `hrfdecoder` design API: rMVPA's core infrastructure expects `mvpa_design` fields like `train_design`, `y_train`, etc. continuous regression external test domains, fields largely bookkeeping, actual predictors carried explicitly `feature_sets` objects. data live. Predictors live design: `design$X_train` `design$X_test` (`feature_sets`). Responses live dataset: `dataset$train_data` `dataset$test_data` (TRxvoxel matrices per ROI). cv_labels semantics. returned object passes `cv_labels = 1:T_enc` `mvpa_design()`. integer indices used length validation fold-construction bookkeeping generic rMVPA iterators; meaningful training targets. actual predictors carried `design$X_train` / `design$X_test`, models built design (e.g. `grouped_ridge_da_model()` / `banded_ridge_da_model()`) retrieve fields rather `cv_labels`. Recall blocks. `block_var_test` stored convenience `design$block_var_test`. Models can use define blocked cross-validation test/target time (e.g. leave-one-run-), fall back contiguous folds single run present. Fold-aware target builders. unbiased domain-adaptation workflows, `feature_sets_design()` can store `target_builder` callback instead single fixed `X_test`. callback invoked separately outer target fold receives source predictors plus target train/test row indices. must return target-side predictors original target row order, either `feature_sets` object, numeric matrix, list containing `gamma`, `X`, `X_test`. allows upstream matching/alignment re-fit target-train rows , held-target rows remain untouched evaluation.

title: Feature-sets design (mvpa_design extension for continuous regression) — feature_sets_design
code: # Train predictors (TR x features), split into named sets: X_enc <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 8), 20, 8) fs_enc <- feature_sets(X_enc, blocks(low = 3, sem = 5))  # Test predictors (TR x features), for example from a soft alignment: gamma <- matrix(runif(10 * 20), 10, 20) gamma <- gamma / rowSums(gamma) fs_rec <- expected_features(fs_enc, gamma, drop_null = FALSE, renormalize = TRUE)  des <- feature_sets_design(fs_enc, fs_rec, block_var_test = rep(1:2, each = 5))

title: Haufe Feature Importance (Activation Patterns) — haufe_importance
text: Computes per-feature importance using Haufe et al. (2014) transformation decoding weights encoding (activation) patterns: = Sigma_x %*% W %*% solve(t(W) %*% Sigma_x %*% W).

title: Haufe Feature Importance (Activation Patterns) — haufe_importance
code: haufe_importance(W, Sigma_x, summary_fun = function(A) sqrt(rowSums(A^2)))

title: Haufe Feature Importance (Activation Patterns) — haufe_importance
text: W P x D weight matrix (features x discriminant directions). Sigma_x P x P covariance matrix training features. summary_fun function applied rows produce scalar importance per feature. Defaults L2 norm across discriminants.

title: Haufe Feature Importance (Activation Patterns) — haufe_importance
text: list components: P x D activation pattern matrix. importance numeric vector length P per-feature importance.

title: Haufe Feature Importance (Activation Patterns) — haufe_importance

what: Construct an ITEM design for trial-wise decoding — item_design
title: Construct an ITEM design for trial-wise decoding — item_design
text: Creates design object ITEM-style decoding trial-wise design matrix (`X_t`) supervised trial targets (`T_target`) decoupled TR-level observations used searchlight/ROI extraction.

title: Construct an ITEM design for trial-wise decoding — item_design
code: item_design(   train_design = NULL,   X_t,   T_target,   run_id,   C_transform = NULL,   V = NULL,   v_type = c("cov", "precision"),   trial_id = NULL,   trial_hash = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   Z = NULL,   Nuisance = NULL,   meta = list(),   ... )

title: Construct an ITEM design for trial-wise decoding — item_design
text: train_design Data frame/tibble describing TR-level observations. `NULL`, minimal design one row per TR created. X_t Numeric trial-wise design matrix (`n_time x n_trials`). T_target Trial-level supervised targets (matrix/data.frame/vector/factor). Length/rows must equal `n_trials`. run_id Trial-level run/session identifiers (length `n_trials`). C_transform Optional trial--condition transform matrix. V Optional temporal covariance/precision passed `fmrilss::item_compute_u()`. v_type One `"cov"` `"precision"`. trial_id Optional trial identifiers. trial_hash Optional trial hash used alignment guards. split_by Optional split variable passed `mvpa_design()`. Z Optional nuisance matrix (`n_time x q`) used LS-(`fmrilss::lsa`). Nuisance Alias nuisance matrix LS-; provided, `Z` used. meta Optional metadata list. ... Reserved forward compatibility.

title: Construct an ITEM design for trial-wise decoding — item_design
text: object class `c("item_design", "mvpa_design", "list")`.

text: Integrates ITEM-style trial-wise decoding `fit_roi` architecture delegating trial-level estimation covariance-aware decoding `fmrilss`.

title: ITEM decoding model for ROI/searchlight analysis — item_model
code: item_model(   dataset,   design,   mode = c("classification", "regression"),   metric = NULL,   ridge_u = 0,   ridge_w = 1e-04,   lsa_method = c("r", "cpp"),   solver = c("chol", "svd", "pinv"),   u_storage = c("matrix", "by_run"),   class_levels = NULL,   check_hash = FALSE,   return_predictions = TRUE,   compute_performance = TRUE,   ... )

title: ITEM decoding model for ROI/searchlight analysis — item_model
text: dataset `mvpa_dataset`. design `item_design` object. mode Decoding mode: `"classification"` `"regression"`. metric Optional ITEM metric. Classification: `"accuracy"`, `"balanced_accuracy"`. Regression: `"correlation"`, `"rmse"`. ridge_u Non-negative ridge used computing `U`. ridge_w Non-negative ridge used fitting ITEM weights per fold. lsa_method LS-backend `fmrilss::lsa()` (`"r"` `"cpp"`). solver Solver preference ITEM linear solves (`"chol"`, `"svd"`, `"pinv"`). u_storage Store trial covariance full matrix (`"matrix"`) run blocks (`"by_run"`). class_levels Optional class order classification. check_hash Validate trial hash CV available. return_predictions Keep trial-level prediction tables ROI results. compute_performance Reserved API compatibility. ITEM always computes scalar ROI metrics mapping. ... Additional fields stored model spec.

title: ITEM decoding model for ROI/searchlight analysis — item_model
text: model spec class `item_model` compatible   `run_regional()` `run_searchlight()`.

title: ITEM decoding model for ROI/searchlight analysis — item_model
text: Conceptually, ITEM differs archived `hrfdecoder` pathway decoder fit: - ITEM (`item_model`) first estimates trial-wise responses (`Gamma`) via LS-  performs covariance-aware decoding trial estimates. - archived `hrfdecoder` model fits continuous-time decoder directly   TR-level data aggregates TR predictions events afterward. Use ITEM want explicit trial-estimation stage direct control trial covariance handling (`U`), especially trial-level diagnostics.

what: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
title: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
text: Creates contrasts based matrix rows represent conditions columns represent features (e.g., neural network embeddings, semantic features). Optionally performs PCA reduce dimensionality.

title: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
code: make_feature_contrasts(   features,   labels = NULL,   use_pca = TRUE,   centre_pca = TRUE,   scale_pca = FALSE,   pve = 0.9,   n_pcs = NULL,   prefix = "Feat_" )

title: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
text: features numeric matrix (K x P) K number conditions P number features. Row names, present, correspond condition labels. Column names recommended. labels Optional character vector condition labels. provided, rows `features` matrix reordered match order. NULL, order `rownames(features)` used (available). use_pca Logical. TRUE (default), performs Principal Component Analysis (PCA) features. FALSE, uses raw features directly. centre_pca Logical. `use_pca = TRUE`, features centered PCA? (Default: TRUE) Note: Reordering rows based `labels` argument happens ** PCA. scale_pca Logical. `use_pca = TRUE`, features scaled unit variance PCA? (Default: FALSE, scaling can affect variance explained). Note: Reordering rows based `labels` argument happens ** PCA. pve Numeric (0 1). `use_pca = TRUE`, selects minimum number principal components (PCs) needed explain least proportion variance. Ignored `n_pcs` specified. (Default: 0.9) n_pcs Integer. `use_pca = TRUE`, selects exactly number principal components. Takes precedence `pve`. (Default: NULL) prefix Character string prepend column names output matrix (e.g., "Feat_", "PCA_"). (Default: "Feat_")

title: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
text: numeric matrix (K x Q) K matches number conditions/labels   Q number selected features principal components.   Rows ordered according `labels` `rownames(features)`.   Columns named using `prefix` either original feature names   (`use_pca=FALSE`) component numbers (e.g., "PCA_PC1", "PCA_PC2").

title: Generate Contrasts from a Feature Matrix (Optional PCA) — make_feature_contrasts
code: # Example feature matrix (4 conditions, 5 features) feat_mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 4,                    dimnames = list(paste0("Cond", 1:4), paste0("F", 1:5)))  # Use raw features (first 3) C_raw <- make_feature_contrasts(feat_mat[, 1:3], use_pca = FALSE, prefix="RawFeat_") print(C_raw) #>        RawFeat_F1 RawFeat_F2 RawFeat_F3 #> Cond1  0.07690286 -0.6069563 -1.5540788 #> Cond2  0.55834935 -0.3955575  0.5245265 #> Cond3 -0.82182161  1.2180820 -0.3984093 #> Cond4 -0.36515220  0.6044720 -0.3807170  # Use PCA, selecting top 2 PCs C_pca <- make_feature_contrasts(feat_mat, use_pca = TRUE, n_pcs = 2, prefix="PCA_") print(C_pca) #>             PCA_PC1     PCA_PC2 #> Cond1  0.5348726259 -1.30373417 #> Cond2  1.2396494456  1.01259686 #> Cond3 -1.7747930122  0.31436180 #> Cond4  0.0002709406 -0.02322449  # Use PCA, selecting >= 80% variance explained C_pca_pve <- make_feature_contrasts(feat_mat, use_pca = TRUE, pve = 0.8, prefix="PCA_") print(C_pca_pve) #>             PCA_PC1     PCA_PC2 #> Cond1  0.5348726259 -1.30373417 #> Cond2  1.2396494456  1.01259686 #> Cond3 -1.7747930122  0.31436180 #> Cond4  0.0002709406 -0.02322449  # Reorder based on labels C_pca_reorder <- make_feature_contrasts(feat_mat, labels=c("Cond3", "Cond1", "Cond4", "Cond2"),                                       use_pca = TRUE, n_pcs = 2, prefix="PCA_") print(C_pca_reorder) #>             PCA_PC1     PCA_PC2 #> Cond3  1.7747930122 -0.31436180 #> Cond1 -0.5348726259  1.30373417 #> Cond4 -0.0002709406  0.02322449 #> Cond2 -1.2396494456 -1.01259686

(2014)     forward-model activation patterns: = Sigma_x * W * inv(W' Sigma_x W).     returned vector L2 norm (custom summary_fun)     rows .     recommended importance measure neuroscience interpretation     reflects neural signal originates, merely     features carry discriminative weight. randomForest Returns MeanDecreaseGini (MeanDecreaseAccuracy     available).     backward (decoding) measure     Haufe-safe: suppressor variables reduce node impurity without     carrying neural signal receive high importance.     Use region_importance slower robust     backward measure bounded --sample performance,     restrict neuroscience interpretation linear models Haufe-based     importance. default Returns NULL, signaling importance     available model class. model_importance.randomForest returns Gini-based importance (MeanDecreaseGini) default, permutation-based importance (MeanDecreaseAccuracy) forest trained importance = TRUE. backward (decoding) measures may assign high importance suppressor variables carry neural signal. neuroscience interpretation, prefer haufe_importance linear model use region_importance model-agnostic alternative bounded --sample performance.

what: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
title: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
text: Unified entry point second-order representational connectivity. Accepts matrix/list/tibble per-unit RDM vectors, fitted regional result carrying per-ROI model-space fingerprints / RDM vectors, forwards rdm_model_space_connectivity projection similarity summaries.

title: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
code: model_space_connectivity(result, model_rdms = NULL, ...)  # Default S3 method model_space_connectivity(   result,   model_rdms = NULL,   method = c("pearson", "spearman"),   basis = c("pca", "qr"),   use = c("complete.obs", "everything"),   tol = 1e-08,   return_projected = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'regional_mvpa_result' model_space_connectivity(   result,   model_rdms = NULL,   method = c("pearson", "spearman"),   basis = c("pca", "qr"),   use = c("complete.obs", "everything"),   tol = 1e-08,   return_projected = FALSE,   prefer = c("fingerprint", "rdm"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'searchlight_result' model_space_connectivity(   result,   model_rdms = NULL,   k = 20L,   scale = c("norm", "raw"),   seeds = c("kmeans"),   nstart = 10L,   iter.max = 30L,   random_seed = NULL,   build_maps = TRUE,   ... )

title: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
text: result Either numeric matrix / list / tibble per-unit RDM vectors (forwarded directly rdm_model_space_connectivity), fitted regional rMVPA object carries fingerprints / RDM vectors per ROI (e.g. regional_mvpa_result run_regional() rsa_model(..., return_fingerprint = TRUE) feature_rsa_model(..., return_rdm_vectors = TRUE)). model_rdms Named list square symmetric model RDMs / dist objects, numeric matrix RDM cells rows. Required per-unit summaries RDM vectors. Optional fingerprints already live result: case fingerprints taken ROI--axis matrix F function returns tcrossprod(F) plus decompositions, without re-projecting. ... Additional arguments forwarded underlying methods. method, basis, use, tol, return_projected Forwarded rdm_model_space_connectivity(). See function details. prefer Either "fingerprint" (default) "rdm" representations available result. k Integer; number anchors select. Default 20, clamped number available searchlight centers. scale One "norm" (default; row-normalize fingerprints similarity cosine) "raw" (use raw fingerprint inner products). seeds Anchor selection strategy: "kmeans" (default; cluster fingerprints pick searchlight closest centroid) integer vector explicit center IDs use anchors. nstart, iter.max Forwarded stats::kmeans(). Default nstart = 10, iter.max = 30. random_seed Integer seed reproducible k-means starts. Default NULL (seeding). supplied, caller's global RNG state restored anchor selection. build_maps Logical; TRUE (default) build one NeuroVol/NeuroSurface per anchor via build_output_map. Skip FALSE need numerical similarity matrix.

title: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
text: object class rdm_model_space_connectivity (see   rdm_model_space_connectivity() details). searchlight_result inputs, object class   model_space_anchor_connectivity. anchor summary,   full searchlight--searchlight matrix. contains   n_centers x k similarity matrix, anchor center IDs,   optional anchor maps, clustering metadata k-means anchors   used.

title: Model-space representational connectivity from a fitted rMVPA result — model_space_connectivity
text: seamless front-end advocated pair-observation model-space RSA design: within-unit RSA fits across-unit representational connectivity become two views fitted object. searchlight_result inputs full \(F F^\top\) matrix intentionally never materialized. Instead, k anchor searchlights chosen (default: k-means clustering per-center fingerprint matrix, searchlight closest centroid anchor) connectivity summarized n_centers x k similarity matrix plus one brain map per anchor. Memory \(\mathcal{O}(n_{centers} \cdot k)\) rather \(\mathcal{O}(n_{centers}^2)\).

title: Constructor for msreve_design — msreve_design
code: # Assume 'mvpa_des_obj' is a pre-existing mvpa_design object # e.g. from mvpa_design(data=my_data_frame, formula = ~ condition_labels + run_labels, #                       block_var = "run_labels") # Let\'s say mvpa_des_obj implies 6 conditions based on unique(my_data_frame$condition_labels) K <- 6 # Number of conditions Q <- 2 # Number of contrasts  # Example contrast matrix (K x Q) C_mat <- matrix(c(  # C1: Cond 1,2,3 vs 4,5,6   1,  1,  1, -1, -1, -1,  # C2: Cond 1,2 vs 3 (and 0 for 4,5,6 for simplicity here)   1,  1, -2,  0,  0,  0 ), nrow = K, ncol = Q, byrow = FALSE) colnames(C_mat) <- c("GroupComparison", "SubComparison")  # if (inherits(mvpa_des_obj, "mvpa_design")) { #  design_obj <- msreve_design(mvpa_des_obj, C_mat, name="example_msreve") #  print(design_obj) # } # # # Automatically add pairwise interactions # design_obj_int <- msreve_design(mvpa_des_obj, C_mat, #                                include_interactions = TRUE) # colnames(design_obj_int$contrast_matrix)

what: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
text: Performs multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA) across multiple regions interest (ROIs) using batch processing parallel computation.

title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
code: mvpa_iterate(   mod_spec,   vox_list,   ids = 1:length(vox_list),   batch_size = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   processor = NULL,   analysis_type = c("searchlight", "regional"),   drop_probs = FALSE,   fail_fast = FALSE,   save_rdm_vectors_dir = NULL )

title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
text: mod_spec MVPA model specification object containing dataset analyze, compute_performance (logical indicating whether compute performance metrics), return_predictions (logical indicating whether return predictions). vox_list list voxel indices coordinates defining ROI analyze. ids Vector identifiers ROI analysis. Defaults 1:length(vox_list). batch_size Integer specifying number ROIs process per batch. searchlight analyses default 10% total ROIs. regional analyses default ROIs one batch, unless estimated extraction memory exceed budget set options(rMVPA.regional_mem_budget) (default 2 GB), case batches automatically sized stay within budget. verbose Logical indicating whether print progress messages. Defaults TRUE. TRUE progressr package installed, real-time progress bar shown updates ROI completes – even running parallel future workers. Without progressr, coarse batch-level log messages printed. Install install.packages("progressr") activate per session progressr::handlers(global = TRUE). processor Optional custom processing function. NULL, uses default processor. Must accept parameters (obj, roi, rnum) return tibble. analysis_type Character indicating type analysis. Defaults "searchlight". drop_probs Logical; TRUE, drop per-ROI probability matrices computing metrics. Default FALSE. fail_fast Logical; TRUE, stop immediately first ROI error. Default FALSE. save_rdm_vectors_dir Optional directory writing file-backed feature-RSA RDM vector batches instead retaining vectors memory.

title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
text: tibble containing results ROI columns: result List column analysis results (NULL return_predictions=FALSE). indices List column ROI indices used. performance List column performance metrics (computed). id ROI identifier. error Logical indicating error occurred. error_message Error message applicable. warning Logical indicating warning occurred. warning_message Warning message applicable.

title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
text: function processes ROIs batches manage memory usage. batch: Extracts ROI data dataset. Filters ROIs fewer 2 voxels. Processes ROI using either default custom processor. Combines results across batches.

title: Iterate MVPA Analysis Over Multiple ROIs — mvpa_iterate
code: # \donttest{   ds <- gen_sample_dataset(c(5,5,5), 20, blocks=2, nlevels=2)   cval <- blocked_cross_validation(ds$design$block_var)   mdl <- load_model("sda_notune")   mspec <- mvpa_model(mdl, ds$dataset, ds$design,     "classification", crossval=cval)   sl <- get_searchlight(ds$dataset, radius=3)   vox_iter <- lapply(sl, function(x) x)   results <- mvpa_iterate(mspec, vox_iter[1:5],     ids=seq_along(vox_iter[1:5])) #> INFO [2026-04-25 15:55:57] Processing batch 1/1 (5 ROIs in this batch) #> INFO [2026-04-25 15:55:58]  #> MVPA Iteration Complete #> - Total ROIs: 5 #> - Processed: 5 #> - Skipped: 0 # }

title: Create an MVPA Model — mvpa_model
text: model character string naming model MVPAModels registry, custom model specification list. dataset `mvpa_dataset` instance. design `mvpa_design` instance. model_type character string indicating problem type: "classification" "regression". crossval optional `cross_validation` instance. feature_selector optional `feature_selector` instance. tune_grid optional parameter tuning grid `data.frame`. tune_reps number replications used parameter tuning. relevant `tune_grid` supplied. performance optional custom function computing performance metrics. class_metrics logical flag indicating whether compute performance metrics class. compute_performance logical indicating whether compute store performance measures voxel set (defaults TRUE). return_predictions logical indicating whether return row-wise predictions voxel set (defaults TRUE). return_fits logical indicating whether return model fit voxel set (defaults FALSE).

title: Create an MVPA Model — mvpa_model
text: `mvpa_model` object (list class 'mvpa_model').

title: Create an MVPA Model — mvpa_model
text: `performance` supplied, must function takes one argument returns named list scalar values. argument function takes class deriving `classification_result` appropriate problem hand. See example .

title: Create an MVPA Model — mvpa_model
code: mod <- load_model("sda") arr_data <- array(rnorm(6*6*6*100), c(6,6,6,100)) sp <- neuroim2::NeuroSpace(c(6,6,6,100)) traindat <- neuroim2::NeuroVec(arr_data, sp) mask <- neuroim2::LogicalNeuroVol(array(rnorm(6*6*6)>-.2, c(6,6,6)), neuroim2::NeuroSpace(c(6,6,6)))  mvdset <- mvpa_dataset(traindat,mask=mask) design <- data.frame(fac=rep(letters[1:4], 25), block=rep(1:10, each=10)) cval <- blocked_cross_validation(design$block) mvdes <- mvpa_design(design, y_train = ~ fac, block_var = ~ block)  custom_perf <- function(result) {   c(accuracy=sum(result$observed == result$predicted)/length(result$observed)) } mvpmod <- mvpa_model(mod, dataset=mvdset, design=mvdes, crossval=cval, performance=custom_perf) print(mvpmod) #> mvpa_model object.  #> model:  sda  #> model type:  classification  #>  #>  Blocked Cross-Validation  #>  #> - Dataset Information  #>   - Observations:  100  #>   - Number of Folds:  10  #> - Block Information  #>   - Total Blocks:  10  #>   - Mean Block Size:  10  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Block Sizes:  1: 10, 2: 10, 3: 10, 4: 10, 5: 10, 6: 10, 7: 10, 8: 10, 9: 10, 10: 10  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Dataset  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Dimensions:  6 x 6 x 6 x 100 observations  #>   - Type:  DenseNeuroVec  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Mask Information  #>   - Areas:  TRUE : 118  #>   - Active voxels/vertices:  118  #>  #>  #>  MVPA Design  #>  #> - Training Data  #>   - Observations:  100  #>   - Response Type:  Factor #>   - Levels:  a, b, c, d  #>   - Class Distribution:  a: 25, b: 25, c: 25, d: 25  #> - Test Data  #>   -  None  #> - Structure  #>   - Blocking:  Present #>   - Number of Blocks:  10  #>   - Mean Block Size:  10  (SD:  0 )  #>   - Split Groups:  None  #>

what: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
text: rMVPA can extended external packages defining S3 model classes inherit model_spec. preferred extension path modern fit_roi/output_schema contract.

title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
text: 1. **Constructor**    Build model object create_model_spec, assigning    model class name (example "my_model"). 2. **fit_roi.my_model (required)**    Implement per-ROI work return roi_result. 3. **output_schema.my_model (recommended)**    Return named list values "scalar" "vector[N]".    enables schema-driven map construction searchlight analyses. 4. **Optional overrides**    - y_train.my_model / y_test.my_model labels      available attached design object.    - strip_dataset.my_model advanced parallel memory control.

title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
text: - mock_roi_data builds lightweight ROI payloads   unit tests. - mock_context builds standard fit_roi   context list. - validate_plugin_model runs contract check one ROI   validates schema/metric agreement. - validate_model_spec performs plugin-readiness lint   optional dry-run execution.

title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
text: rMVPA exposes two different extension registries: Classifier registry Use register_mvpa_model add   new low-level estimator (fit/predict/prob) plugs   built-mvpa_model analysis type. Analysis-type registry Define new S3 model class (via   create_model_spec + fit_roi.<class>) need   new per-ROI analysis workflow, just new estimator backend.

title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
text: - process_roi.default automatically prefers fit_roi   method exists class. - Legacy train_model/process_roi extension paths still   available backward compatibility, new plugins use   fit_roi.

title: Extending rMVPA: Plugin Analysis Models — new-analysis-overview
code: my_model <- function(dataset, design, alpha = 0.1, ...) {   create_model_spec(     "my_model",     dataset = dataset,     design = design,     alpha = alpha,     ...   ) }  fit_roi.my_model <- function(model, roi_data, context, ...) {   score <- mean(roi_data$train_data)   roi_result(     metrics = c(score = score),     indices = roi_data$indices,     id = context$id   ) }  output_schema.my_model <- function(model) {   list(score = "scalar") }  # optional only when you do not rely on design defaults: # y_train.my_model <- function(obj) y_train(obj$design)

what: Preprocess Maps for Spatial NMF — nmf_preprocess_maps
title: Preprocess Maps for Spatial NMF — nmf_preprocess_maps
text: Transforms list neuroimaging maps ensure non-negativity NMF. NMF requires non-negative input data; function provides common transformations different data types.

title: Preprocess Maps for Spatial NMF — nmf_preprocess_maps
code: nmf_preprocess_maps(   maps,   method = c("shift", "auc", "auc_raw", "zscore", "relu", "abs"),   min_val = 0,   floor = -0.5,   mask = NULL )

title: Preprocess Maps for Spatial NMF — nmf_preprocess_maps
text: maps list NeuroVol NeuroSurface objects. method Preprocessing method: "shift" Shifts values minimum becomes `min_val` (default 0).     Use data arbitrary negative values. "auc" AUC values centered chance (.e., AUC - 0.5, ranging     -0.5 0.5). Shifts 0.5 chance becomes 0 perfect     classification becomes 0.5.     Values `floor` (default -0.5) clamped. "auc_raw" raw AUC values (0 1). Subtracts 0.5 applies     "auc" method, chance (0.5) becomes 0. "zscore" z-scored data. Shifts `abs(min) + min_val`. "relu" Clamps negative values zero (rectified linear). "abs" Takes absolute value data. min_val Minimum value transformation (default 0). small positive value (e.g., 0.01) can help numerical stability. floor "auc" method, values clamped (default -0.5). mask Optional mask; provided, statistics computed within mask.

title: Preprocess Maps for Spatial NMF — nmf_preprocess_maps

title: Orthogonalize a Contrast Matrix — orthogonalize_contrasts
text: Orthogonalizes columns contrast matrix using QR decomposition. resulting matrix orthonormal columns spanning space original columns, rank input matrix. Sign output columns heuristically aligned input columns.

title: Orthogonalize a Contrast Matrix — orthogonalize_contrasts
code: orthogonalize_contrasts(C)

title: Orthogonalize a Contrast Matrix — orthogonalize_contrasts
text: C numeric matrix (K x Q) columns represent contrast vectors.

title: Orthogonalize a Contrast Matrix — orthogonalize_contrasts
text: orthogonalized matrix. input matrix C rank-deficient   (rank < Q), output matrix Q columns, first   rank(C) columns non-zero form orthonormal basis;   subsequent columns zero vectors. Column names C preserved.

title: Orthogonalize a Contrast Matrix — orthogonalize_contrasts
code: K <- 6 # Number of conditions Q <- 2 # Number of contrasts C_orig <- matrix(c( 1,  1,  1, -1, -1, -1,  # Contrast 1                     1, -1,  0,  1, -1,  0), # Contrast 2 (not orthogonal to C1)                  nrow=K, ncol=Q) colnames(C_orig) <- c("MainEffect", "InteractionLike") C_ortho <- orthogonalize_contrasts(C_orig) # print(round(crossprod(C_ortho), 10)) # Should be close to identity matrix  # Example with a rank-deficient matrix (3rd contrast is sum of first two) C_rank_def <- cbind(C_orig, C_orig[,1] + C_orig[,2]) colnames(C_rank_def) <- c("C1", "C2", "C3_dependent") C_ortho_def <- orthogonalize_contrasts(C_rank_def) #> Warning: Input matrix C is rank-deficient (rank 2 < 3 columns). The output matrix has 3 columns, but only the first 2 are non-zero and form an orthonormal basis. Subsequent columns are zero vectors. # print(round(crossprod(C_ortho_def), 10)) # The 3rd column of C_ortho_def will be zeros.

what: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
text: Generalizes rsa_design support arbitrary pair-observation geometries: lower-triangle within-domain pairs (classical RSA layout), rectangular -domain pairs (e.g. items domain vs. items domain B), function-valued model RDM entries. Function entries may either operate item identifiers, item identifiers plus feature rows features_a / features_b supplied.

title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
code: pair_rsa_design(   items_a,   items_b = NULL,   model = list(),   nuisance = list(),   pairs = c("within", "between"),   features_a = NULL,   features_b = NULL,   block_var_a = NULL,   block_var_b = NULL,   keep_intra_run = FALSE,   row_idx_a = NULL,   row_idx_b = NULL )

title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
text: items_a Vector item identifiers domain . Length defines n_a. items_b Optional vector item identifiers domain B. Required pairs = ""; ignored otherwise. Length defines n_b. model Named list model RDM specifications. entry may dist object, numeric square matrix (within mode) n_a x n_b matrix (mode), numeric vector length n_pairs, function function(, b) returning numeric vector pairwise values requested pair table. features_a supplied function least four formal arguments (...), called function(, b, features_a, features_b) feature arguments row-aligned pair tables. nuisance Optional named list nuisance pair predictors using accepted forms model. Included RSA design matrix excluded model-space fingerprints returned rsa_model(..., return_fingerprint = TRUE). pairs Either "within" (default) "". features_a Optional data frame matrix item features items_a. Function-valued model/nuisance entries can use rows define feature-pair dissimilarities. features_b Optional data frame matrix item features items_b. Defaults features_a within mode. block_var_a Optional vector block labels (length n_a). supplied keep_intra_run = FALSE, within-block pairs excluded. block_var_b Optional vector block labels domain B, length n_b. Defaults block_var_a within mode. keep_intra_run Logical; TRUE, drop within-block pairs. row_idx_a Optional integer vector dataset row indices corresponding items_a. within mode, lets pair design address subset/reordering dataset rows. Required pairs = "" per-ROI engine can extract correct neural sub-blocks. row_idx_b Integer vector dataset row indices items_b. Required pairs = "".

title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
text: list class c("pair_rsa_design", "rsa_design", "list")   containing fields produced rsa_design() plus pair_kind Either "within" "". items_a, items_b Item identifier vectors. n_a, n_b Item counts. pair_index data frame describing retained pair. row_idx_a, row_idx_b Dataset row indices pairs = "".

title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
text: returned object inherits rsa_design drop-replacement used rsa_model regional / searchlight engines. Within-domain pair designs fully interoperable existing train_model.rsa_model path; -domain designs dispatched pair_kind field, causes train_model.rsa_model compute rectangular neural-pair dissimilarity block instead lower triangle.

title: Construct a pair-observation RSA design — pair_rsa_design
code: set.seed(1) items <- paste0("item", 1:8) R1 <- as.matrix(dist(matrix(rnorm(8 * 4), 8, 4))) R2 <- as.matrix(dist(matrix(rnorm(8 * 4), 8, 4))) rownames(R1) <- colnames(R1) <- rownames(R2) <- colnames(R2) <- items des <- pair_rsa_design(items, model = list(rdm1 = R1, rdm2 = R2)) lengths(des$model_mat) #> rdm1 rdm2  #>   28   28

what: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
title: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
text: Specifies tuning parameters run_permutation_searchlight.

title: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
code: permutation_control(   n_perm = 5L,   shuffle = c("within_block", "circular_shift", "global"),   null_method = c("adjusted", "global"),   adjust_by = c("nfeatures", "redundancy", "both"),   n_bins = 5L,   subsample = 0.1,   stratify_subsample = TRUE,   correction = c("fdr", "none"),   diagnose = TRUE,   seed = NULL,   perm_strategy = c("iterate", "searchlight") )

title: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
text: n_perm Integer >= 1. Number permutations run. shuffle Character. permute labels: "within_block" (default) shuffles within block, "circular_shift" shifts label sequence within block, "global" shuffles labels ignoring block structure. null_method Character. build null distribution: "adjusted" conditions covariate bins (default), "global" uses one global null. adjust_by Character. covariate(s) condition : "nfeatures" (default), "redundancy", "". n_bins Integer >= 2. Number quantile bins covariate stratification. subsample Fraction (0, 1] integer count searchlight centers use permutation runs.  used perm_strategy = "iterate" (see ). stratify_subsample Logical. TRUE (default), subsample centers proportionally nfeatures quantile bins. correction Character. Multiple-comparison correction: "fdr" (Benjamini-Hochberg, default) "none". diagnose Logical. TRUE (default), run null diagnostics. seed Optional integer random seed. perm_strategy Character. Controls permutation pass   executed.  Two strategies available; neither contains   engine-specific branching:  "iterate" (default) permutation runs       mvpa_iterate subsampled set centers.       universal, safe path: works every model type       every searchlight engine goes generic       per-ROI iterator. use: slow classifiers, large brains, limited       compute.  subsample parameter controls many centers       evaluated per permutation, giving 5–20\(\times\) speedup       full-brain pass. Null pool size: n_perm * n_subsampled_centers. "searchlight" permutation runs       run_searchlight full brain,       extracts metric values every center.  call goes       standard run_searchlight dispatch,       automatically benefits fast engine model qualifies       (e.g.\ SWIFT, dual-LDA) well user-defined       run_searchlight.<class> method. use: models fast searchlight engine,       want richest possible null distribution.  Since       full brain computed anyway, centers contribute       null (subsample parameter ignored note       logged). Null pool size: n_perm * all_centers.

title: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
text: S3 object class "permutation_control".

title: Create a Permutation Control Object — permutation_control
code: # Default: subsampled iterator (safe for any model) pc <- permutation_control(n_perm = 100, shuffle = "within_block",                           subsample = 0.1, seed = 42L)  # Full-brain strategy (benefits from fast engines, richer null) pc2 <- permutation_control(n_perm = 20, perm_strategy = "searchlight",                            seed = 42L) print(pc2) #> Permutation Control Settings #>   perm_strategy     : searchlight  #>   n_perm            : 20  #>   shuffle           : within_block  #>   null_method       : adjusted  #>   adjust_by         : nfeatures  #>   n_bins            : 5  #>   subsample         : (ignored - full brain per perm) #>   correction        : fdr  #>   diagnose          : TRUE  #>   seed              : 42

code: # \donttest{   ds <- gen_sample_dataset(c(4, 4, 4), 20, blocks = 2)   cv <- blocked_cross_validation(ds$design$block_var)   mdl <- load_model("sda_notune")   spec <- mvpa_model(     model = mdl,     dataset = ds$dataset,     design = ds$design,     model_type = "classification",     crossval = cv   )   vox <- sample(which(ds$dataset$mask > 0), 30)   samp <- data_sample(ds$dataset, vox)   roi_obj <- as_roi(samp, ds$dataset)   process_roi(spec, roi_obj, 1) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   result         indices    performance    id error error_message warning #>   <list>         <list>     <list>      <dbl> <lgl> <chr>         <lgl>   #> 1 <mltwy_c_ [6]> <dbl [30]> <dbl [2]>       1 FALSE ~             FALSE   #> # ℹ 1 more variable: warning_message <chr> # }

what: rMVPA: A package for multi-voxel pattern analysis (MVPA) — rMVPA
title: rMVPA: A package for multi-voxel pattern analysis (MVPA) — rMVPA
text: rMVPA package provides comprehensive suite tools advanced neuroimaging data analysis using multi-voxel pattern analysis (MVPA). supports various techniques including region--interest (ROI) searchlight analyses. Key functionalities cover: Classification-based MVPA Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA), including standard RSA,         vector-based RSA, feature-based RSA. Contrast RSA (MS-ReVE style analyses) Flexible cross-validation schemes Feature selection methods Tools constructing managing MVPA datasets designs.

what: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
text: Estimates layer- model-specific RDM score vectors removing shared representational structure across set model RDMs. intended preprocessing step RSA several model RDMs strongly correlated goal evaluate distinct representational components.

title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
code: rdm_decorrelate(   rdms,   method = c("ordered_innovation", "zca"),   similarity = c("spearman", "pearson"),   objective = c("constraint", "penalty", "full_residual"),   epsilon = 0.05,   gamma_grid = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05),   mu = 1,   target = c("mean_abs", "mean_squared"),   return = c("dist", "matrix", "vector"),   project_correlation = FALSE,   zca_ridge = 1e-08,   max_combinations = 2e+05,   tol = 1e-10 )

title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
text: rdms Named list square symmetric matrices `dist` objects. RDMs must size contain finite lower-triangular values. method Character string, either `"ordered_innovation"` `"zca"`. similarity Character string. `"spearman"` rank-standardizes RDM vector decorrelation. `"pearson"` uses centered/scaled numeric RDM values directly. objective Character string. `"constraint"` maximizes preservation subject requested decorrelation tolerance feasible. `"penalty"` minimizes preservation loss plus `mu` times decorrelation penalty. `"full_residual"` uses fully residualized ordered innovations ignores `epsilon`. epsilon Non-negative tolerance mean pairwise adjusted-RDM association `objective = "constraint"`. gamma_grid Numeric vector `[0, 1]` used ordered innovation shrinkage. first RDM fixed `gamma = 1`. mu Non-negative penalty multiplier used `objective = "penalty"`. target Character string. `"mean_abs"` penalizes mean absolute pairwise correlation; `"mean_squared"` penalizes mean squared pairwise correlation. return Character string. `"dist"` returns adjusted RDMs `dist` objects, `"matrix"` returns symmetric matrices, `"vector"` returns lower-triangular vectors. project_correlation Logical. `TRUE`, adjusted RDM-score matrix interpreted `1 - correlation`, projected nearest positive semidefinite correlation matrix unit diagonal using `Matrix::nearPD`, converted back RDM. default projection can reintroduce RDM correlations. zca_ridge Numeric ridge added RDM-vector covariance rank deficient `method = "zca"`. max_combinations Maximum number exhaustive `gamma_grid` combinations ordered innovation. Larger problems use coordinate search. tol Numerical tolerance degeneracy rank checks.

title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
text: object class `rdm_decorrelation_result` adjusted RDMs,   lower-triangular vectors, selected shrinkage parameters, diagnostics.

title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
text: function works lower-triangular RDM-vector space. `method = "ordered_innovation"`, RDMs processed order supplied: RDM vector decomposed component predicted earlier innovation vectors residual innovation. shrinkage parameter `gamma` controls much predicted/shared component retained. default objective constrained preservation: preserve much original standardized RDM vector possible forcing mean pairwise association among adjusted RDM vectors `epsilon` feasible. `method = "zca"`, standardized RDM vectors symmetrically whitened. gives exact joint decorrelation Pearson sense RDM-vector covariance matrix full rank, output RDM linear mixture original RDMs. Ordinary identity shrinkage correlation matrix change rank-based RSA correlations among -diagonal RDM vectors, except degenerate endpoint. function instead shrinks shared RDM-vector components.

title: Decorrelation of correlated model RDMs — rdm_decorrelate
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(rnorm(12 * 4), 12, 4) r1 <- dist(X) r2 <- dist(X + matrix(rnorm(12 * 4, sd = 0.2), 12, 4)) dec <- rdm_decorrelate(list(layer1 = r1, layer2 = r2), epsilon = 0.05) dec$mean_abs_cor_after #> [1] 8.121685e-16

what: ROI-by-ROI similarity through a correlated model-RDM space — rdm_model_space_connectivity
title: ROI-by-ROI similarity through a correlated model-RDM space — rdm_model_space_connectivity
